translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blessed is the man that walketh not in the counsel of the wicked, and standeth not in the way of sinners, and sitteth not in the seat of scorners;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But his delight is in Jehovah's law, and in his law doth he meditate day and night.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he [is] as a tree planted by brooks of water, which giveth its fruit in its season, and whose leaf fadeth not; and all that he doeth prospereth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The wicked are not so; but are as the chaff which the wind driveth away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore the wicked shall not stand in the judgment, nor sinners in the assembly of the righteous.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For Jehovah knoweth the way of the righteous, but the way of the wicked shall perish.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Why are the nations in tumultuous agitation, and [why] do the peoples meditate a vain thing?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The kings of the earth set themselves, and the princes plot together, against Jehovah and against his anointed:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let us break their bonds asunder, and cast away their cords from us!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that dwelleth in the heavens shall laugh, the Lord shall have them in derision.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then will he speak to them in his anger, and in his fierce displeasure will he terrify them:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I have anointed my king upon Zion, the hill of my holiness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will declare the decree: Jehovah hath said unto me, Thou art my Son; I this day have begotten thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ask of me, and I will give thee nations for an inheritance, and for thy possession the ends of the earth:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou shalt break them with a sceptre of iron, as a potter's vessel thou shalt dash them in pieces.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, O kings, be ye wise, be admonished, ye judges of the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Serve Jehovah with fear, and rejoice with trembling.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Kiss the Son, lest he be angry, and ye perish in the way, though his anger burn but a little. Blessed are all who have their trust in him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah, how many are they that trouble me, many they that rise up against me!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Many say of my soul, There is no salvation for him inGod. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But thou, Jehovah, art a shield about me; my glory, and the lifter up of my head.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: With my voice will I call to Jehovah, and he will answer me from the hill of his holiness. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I laid me down and slept; I awaked, for Jehovah sustaineth me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will not fear for myriads of the people that have set themselves against me round about.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Arise, Jehovah; save me, myGod! For thou hast smitten all mine enemies upon the cheekbone, thou hast broken the teeth of the wicked.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Salvation is of Jehovah; thy blessing is upon thy people. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When I call, answer me, OGod of my righteousness: in pressure thou hast enlarged me; be gracious unto me, and hear my prayer.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye sons of men, till when is my glory [to be put] to shame? [How long] will ye love vanity, will ye seek after a lie? Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But know that Jehovah hath set apart the pious [man] for himself: Jehovah will hear when I call unto him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be moved with anger, and sin not; meditate in your own hearts upon your bed, and be still. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Offer sacrifices of righteousness, and confide in Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Many say, Who shall cause us to see good? Lift up upon us the light of thy countenance, O Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast put joy in my heart, more than in the time that their corn and their new wine was in abundance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In peace will I both lay me down and sleep; for thou, Jehovah, alone makest me to dwell in safety.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Give ear to my words, O Jehovah; consider my meditation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hearken unto the voice of my crying, my king and myGod; for to thee will I pray.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah, in the morning shalt thou hear my voice; in the morning will I address myself to thee, and will look up.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thou art not aGod that hath pleasure in wickedness; evil shall not sojourn with thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Insolent fools shall not stand before thine eyes; thou hatest all workers of iniquity.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou wilt destroy them that speak lies: Jehovah abhorreth a man of blood and deceit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But as for me, in the greatness of thy loving-kindness will I enter thy house; I will bow down toward the temple of thy holiness in thy fear.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Lead me, Jehovah, in thy righteousness, because of my foes; make thy way plain before me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For there is no certainty in their mouth; their inward part is perversion, their throat is an open sepulchre; they flatter with their tongue.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Bring guilt upon them, OGod; let them fall by their own counsels: drive them out in the multitude of their transgressions, for they have rebelled against thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all that trust in thee shall rejoice: for ever shall they shout joyously, and thou wilt protect them; and they that love thy name shall exult in thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thou, Jehovah, wilt bless the righteous [man]; with favour wilt thou surround him as [with] a shield.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah, rebuke me not in thine anger, and chasten me not in thy hot displeasure.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be gracious unto me, Jehovah, for I am withered; Jehovah, heal me, for my bones tremble.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And my soul trembleth exceedingly: and thou, Jehovah, till how long?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Return, Jehovah, free my soul; save me for thy loving-kindness' sake.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For in death there is no remembrance of thee; in Sheol who shall give thanks unto thee?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I am wearied with my groaning; all the night make I my bed to swim; I dissolve my couch with my tears.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Mine eye wasteth away through grief; it hath grown old because of all mine oppressors.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Depart from me, all ye workers of iniquity; for Jehovah hath heard the voice of my weeping.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah hath heard my supplication; Jehovah receiveth my prayer.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All mine enemies shall be ashamed and tremble exceedingly; they will turn, they will be ashamed suddenly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah myGod, in thee have I trusted: save me from all my pursuers, and deliver me;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Lest he tear my soul like a lion, crushing it while there is no deliverer.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah myGod, if I have done this, if there be iniquity in my hands;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If I have rewarded evil to him that was at peace with me; (indeed I have freed him that without cause oppressed me;)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let the enemy pursue after my soul, and take [it], and let him tread down my life to the earth, and lay my glory in the dust. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Arise, Jehovah, in thine anger; lift thyself up against the raging of mine oppressors, and awake for me: thou hast commanded judgment.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the assembly of the peoples shall encompass thee; and for their sakes return thou on high.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah shall minister judgment to the peoples. Judge me, Jehovah, according to my righteousness, and according to mine integrity which is in me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Oh let the wrong of the wicked come to an end, and establish thou the righteous [man]; even thou that triest the hearts and reins, the righteousGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My shield is withGod, who saveth the upright in heart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: God is a righteous judge, and aGod who is indignant all the day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If one turn not, he will sharpen his sword; he hath bent his bow and made it ready,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he hath prepared for him instruments of death; his arrows hath he made burning.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, he travaileth with iniquity, yea, he hath conceived mischief, and brought forth falsehood:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He digged a pit, and hollowed it out, and is fallen into the hole that he made.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His mischief shall return upon his own head, and his violence shall come down upon his own pate.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will praise Jehovah according to his righteousness, and will sing forth the name of Jehovah the Most High.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah our Lord, how excellent is thy name in all the earth! who hast set thy majesty above the heavens.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Out of the mouth of babes and sucklings hast thou established praise because of thine adversaries, to still the enemy and the avenger.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When I see thy heavens, the work of thy fingers, the moon and stars, which thou hast established;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: What is man, that thou art mindful of him? and the son of man, that thou visitest him?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast made him a little lower than the angels, and hast crowned him with glory and splendour.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast made him to rule over the works of thy hands; thou hast put everything under his feet:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Sheep and oxen, all of them, and also the beasts of the field;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The fowl of the heavens, and the fishes of the sea, [whatever] passeth through the paths of the seas.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah our Lord, how excellent is thy name in all the earth!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will praise Jehovah with my whole heart; I will recount all thy marvellous works.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will be glad and rejoice in thee; I will sing forth thy name, O Most High.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When mine enemies turned back, they stumbled and perished at thy presence:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thou hast maintained my right and my cause. Thou sittest on the throne, judging righteously.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast rebuked the nations, thou hast destroyed the wicked; thou hast put out their name for ever and ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: O enemy! destructions are ended for ever. — Thou hast also destroyed cities, even the remembrance of them hath perished.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jehovah sitteth for ever; he hath ordained his throne for judgment.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it is he that will judge the world with righteousness; he shall execute judgment upon the peoples with equity.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah will be a refuge to the oppressed one, a refuge in times of distress.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they that know thy name will confide in thee; for thou, Jehovah, hast not forsaken them that seek thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Sing psalms to Jehovah who dwelleth in Zion; tell among the peoples his doings.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For when he maketh inquisition for blood, he remembereth them; the cry of the afflicted ones hath he not forgotten.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be gracious unto me, O Jehovah; consider mine affliction from them that hate me, lifting me up from the gates of death:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: That I may declare all thy praise in the gates of the daughter of Zion. I will be joyful in thy salvation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The nations are sunk down in the pit [that] they made; in the net that they hid is their own foot taken.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah is known [by] the judgment he hath executed: the wicked is ensnared in the work of his own hands. Higgaion. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The wicked shall be turned into Sheol, all the nations that forgetGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the needy one shall not be forgotten alway; the hope of the meek shall not perish for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Arise, Jehovah; let not man prevail: let the nations be judged in thy sight.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Put them in fear, Jehovah: that the nations may know themselves to be but men. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Why, Jehovah, standest thou afar off? [Why] hidest thou thyself in times of distress?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The wicked, in his pride, doth hotly pursue the afflicted. They shall be taken in the devices that they have imagined.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the wicked boasteth of his soul's desire, and he blesseth the covetous; he contemneth Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The wicked [saith], in the haughtiness of his countenance, He doth not search out: all his thoughts are, There is noGod!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His ways always succeed; thy judgments are far above out of his sight; [as for] all his adversaries, he puffeth at them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He saith in his heart, I shall not be moved; from generation to generation I shall be in no adversity.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His mouth is full of cursing, and deceit, and oppression; under his tongue is mischief and iniquity.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He sitteth in the lurking-places of the villages; in the secret places doth he slay the innocent: his eyes watch for the wretched.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He lieth in wait secretly, like a lion in his thicket; he lieth in wait to catch the afflicted: he doth catch the afflicted, drawing him into his net.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He croucheth, he boweth down, that the wretched may fall by his strong ones.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He saith in his heart,God hath forgotten, he hideth his face, he will never see [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Arise, Jehovah; OGod, lift up thy hand: forget not the afflicted.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wherefore doth the wicked contemnGod? He hath said in his heart, Thou wilt not require [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast seen [it], for thou thyself beholdest trouble and vexation, to requite by thy hand. The wretched committeth himself unto thee; thou hast been the helper of the fatherless.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Break thou the arm of the wicked, and as for the evil man, seek out his wickedness [till] thou find none.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah is King for ever and ever: the nations have perished out of his land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah, thou hast heard the desire of the meek, thou hast established their heart: thou causest thine ear to hear,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: To do justice to the fatherless and the oppressed one, that the man of the earth may terrify no more.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In Jehovah have I put my trust: how say ye to my soul, Flee [as] a bird to your mountain?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For behold, the wicked bend the bow, they make ready their arrow upon the string, that they may in darkness shoot at the upright in heart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If the foundations be destroyed, what shall the righteous do?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah [is] in the temple of his holiness; Jehovah, — his throne is in the heavens: his eyes behold, his eyelids try the children of men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah trieth the righteous one; but the wicked, and him that loveth violence, his soul hateth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Upon the wicked he shall rain snares, fire and brimstone; and scorching wind shall be the portion of their cup.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For righteous is Jehovah; he loveth righteousness, his countenance doth behold the upright.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Save, Jehovah, for thegodly man is gone; for the faithful have failed from among the children of men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They speak falsehood every one with his neighbour: [with] flattering lip, with a double heart, do they speak.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah will cut off all flattering lips, the tongue that speaketh proud things,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who have said, With our tongue will we prevail, our lips are our own: who [is] lord over us?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Because of the oppression of the afflicted, because of the sighing of the needy, now will I arise, saith Jehovah, I will set [him] in safety, at whom they puff.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The words of Jehovah are pure words, silver tried in the furnace of earth, purified seven times.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou, Jehovah, wilt keep them, thou wilt preserve them from this generation for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The wicked walk about on every side, when vileness is exalted among the children of men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How long, Jehovah, wilt thou forget me for ever? how long wilt thou hide thy face from me?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How long shall I take counsel in my soul, with sorrow in my heart daily? how long shall mine enemy be exalted over me?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Consider, answer me, O Jehovah myGod! lighten mine eyes, lest I sleep the [sleep of] death;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Lest mine enemy say, I have prevailed against him! [lest] mine adversaries be joyful when I am moved.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As for me, I have confided in thy loving-kindness; my heart shall be joyful in thy salvation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will sing unto Jehovah, for he hath dealt bountifully with me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The fool hath said in his heart, There is noGod. They have corrupted themselves, they have done abominable works: there is none that doeth good.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah looked down from the heavens upon the children of men, to see if there were any that did understand, that did seekGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They have all gone aside, they are together become corrupt: there is none that doeth good, not even one.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Have all the workers of iniquity no knowledge, eating up my people [as] they eat bread? They call not upon Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There were they in great fear; forGod is in the generation of the righteous.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye have shamed the counsel of the afflicted, because Jehovah [was] his refuge.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Oh that the salvation of Israel were come out of Zion! When Jehovah turneth again the captivity of his people, Jacob shall be glad, Israel shall rejoice.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah, who shall sojourn in thy tent? who shall dwell in the hill of thy holiness?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that walketh uprightly, and worketh righteousness, and speaketh the truth from his heart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [He that] slandereth not with his tongue, doeth not evil to his companion, nor taketh up a reproach against his neighbour;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In whose eyes the depraved person is contemned, and who honoureth them that fear Jehovah; who, if he have sworn to his own hurt, changeth it not;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [He that] putteth not out his money to usury, nor taketh reward against the innocent. He that doeth these [things] shall never be moved.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Preserve me, OGod: for I trust in thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou [my soul] hast said to Jehovah, Thou art the Lord: my goodness [extendeth] not to thee; —

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: To the saints that are on the earth, and to the excellent [thou hast said], In them is all my delight.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Their sorrows shall be multiplied that hasten after another: their drink-offerings of blood will I not offer, and I will not take up their names into my lips.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah is the portion of mine inheritance and of my cup: thou maintainest my lot.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The lines are fallen unto me in pleasant places; yea, I have a goodly heritage.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will bless Jehovah, who giveth me counsel; even in the nights my reins instruct me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I have set Jehovah continually before me; because [he is] at my right hand, I shall not be moved.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore my heart rejoiceth, and my glory exulteth; my flesh moreover shall dwell in hope.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thou wilt not leave my soul to Sheol, neither wilt thou allow thy Holy One to see corruption.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou wilt make known to me the path of life: thy countenance is fulness of joy; at thy right hand are pleasures for evermore.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hear the right, O Jehovah, attend unto my cry; give ear unto my prayer, which is not out of feigned lips.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let my judgment come forth from thy presence, let thine eyes regard equity.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast proved my heart, thou hast visited me by night; thou hast tried me, thou hast found nothing: my thought goeth not beyond my word.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Concerning the works of men, by the word of thy lips I have kept from the paths of the violent [man].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When thou holdest my goings in thy paths, my footsteps slip not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I have called upon thee, for thou answerest me, OGod. Incline thine ear unto me, hear my speech.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Shew wondrously thy loving-kindnesses, O thou that savest by thy right hand them that trust [in thee] from those that rise up [against them].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Keep me as the apple of the eye, hide me under the shadow of thy wings,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: From the wicked that destroy me, my deadly enemies, who compass me about.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They are enclosed in their own fat; with their mouth they speak proudly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They have now encompassed us in our steps; their eyes have they set, bowing down to the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He is like a lion that is greedy of its prey, and as a young lion lurking in secret places.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Arise, Jehovah, anticipate him, cast him down: deliver my soul from the wicked, thy sword;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: From men [who are] thy hand, O Jehovah, from men of this age: their portion is in [this] life, and their belly thou fillest with thy hid [treasure]; they have their fill of sons, and leave the rest of their [substance] to their children.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As for me, I will behold thy face in righteousness; I shall be satisfied, when I awake, with thy likeness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will love thee, O Jehovah, my strength.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah is my rock, and my fortress, and my deliverer; myGod, my rock, in whom I will trust; my shield, and the horn of my salvation, my high tower.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will call upon Jehovah, who is to be praised: so shall I be saved from mine enemies.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The bands of death encompassed me, and torrents of Belial made me afraid.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The bands of Sheol surrounded me, the cords of death encountered me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In my distress I called upon Jehovah, and I cried out to myGod; he heard my voice out of his temple, and my cry came before him, into his ears.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then the earth shook and quaked, and the foundations of the mountains trembled and shook, because he was wroth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There went up a smoke out of his nostrils, and fire out of his mouth devoured: coals burned forth from it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he bowed the heavens, and came down; and darkness was under his feet.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he rode upon a cherub and did fly; yea, he flew fast upon the wings of the wind.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He made darkness his secret place, his tent round about him: darkness of waters, thick clouds of the skies.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: From the brightness before him his thick clouds passed forth: hail and coals of fire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah thundered in the heavens, and the Most High uttered his voice: hail and coals of fire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he sent his arrows, and scattered [mine enemies]; and he shot forth lightnings, and discomfited them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the beds of the waters were seen, and the foundations of the world were uncovered at thy rebuke, Jehovah, at the blast of the breath of thy nostrils.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He reached forth from above, he took me, he drew me out of great waters:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He delivered me from my strong enemy, and from them that hated me; for they were mightier than I.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They encountered me in the day of my calamity, but Jehovah was my stay.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he brought me forth into a large place; he delivered me, because he delighted in me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah hath rewarded me according to my righteousness; according to the cleanness of my hands hath he recompensed me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I have kept the ways of Jehovah, and have not wickedly departed from myGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For all his ordinances were before me, and I did not put away his statutes from me;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I was upright with him, and kept myself from mine iniquity.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah hath recompensed me according to my righteousness, according to the cleanness of my hands in his sight.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: With the gracious thou dost shew thyself gracious; with the upright man thou dost shew thyself upright;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: With the pure thou dost shew thyself pure; and with the perverse thou dost shew thyself contrary.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For it is thou that savest the afflicted people; but the haughty eyes wilt thou bring down.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For it is thou that makest my lamp to shine: Jehovah myGod enlighteneth my darkness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For by thee I have run through a troop; and by myGod have I leaped over a wall.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As forGod, his way is perfect; the word of Jehovah is tried: he is a shield to all that trust in him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For who isGod save Jehovah? and who is a rock if not ourGod?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: TheGod who girdeth me with strength, and maketh my way perfect,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who maketh my feet like hinds' [feet], and setteth me upon my high places;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who teacheth my hands to war, and mine arms bend a bow of brass;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou didst give me the shield of thy salvation, and thy right hand held me up; and thy condescending gentleness hath made me great.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou didst enlarge my steps under me, and mine ankles have not wavered.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I pursued mine enemies, and overtook them; and I turned not again till they were consumed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I crushed them, and they were not able to rise: they fell under my feet.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou girdedst me with strength to battle; thou didst subdue under me those that rose up against me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And mine enemies didst thou make to turn their backs unto me, and those that hated me I destroyed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They cried, and there was none to save; — unto Jehovah, and he answered them not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I did beat them small as dust before the wind; I did cast them out as the mire of the streets.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast delivered me from the strivings of the people; thou hast made me the head of the nations: a people I knew not doth serve me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: At the hearing of the ear, they obey me: strangers come cringing unto me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Strangers have faded away, and they come trembling forth from their close places.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah liveth; and blessed be my rock; and exalted be theGod of my salvation,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: TheGod who hath avenged me, and hath subjected the peoples to me;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who hath delivered me from mine enemies: yea, thou hast lifted me up above them that rose up against me; from the man of violence hast thou delivered me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore will I give thanks to thee, Jehovah, among the nations, and will sing psalms to thy name.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [It is he] who giveth great deliverances to his king, and sheweth loving-kindness to his anointed, to David, and to his seed for evermore.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The heavens declare the glory ofGod; and the expanse sheweth the work of his hands.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Day unto day uttereth speech, and night unto night sheweth knowledge.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There is no speech and there are no words, yet their voice is heard.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Their line is gone out through all the earth, and their language to the extremity of the world. In them hath he set a tent for the sun,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he is as a bridegroom going forth from his chamber; he rejoiceth as a strong man to run the race.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His going forth is from the end of the heavens, and his circuit unto the ends of it; and there is nothing hid from the heat thereof.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The law of Jehovah is perfect, restoring the soul; the testimony of Jehovah is sure, making wise the simple;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The precepts of Jehovah are right, rejoicing the heart; the commandment of Jehovah is pure, enlightening the eyes;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The fear of Jehovah is clean, enduring for ever; the judgments of Jehovah are truth, they are righteous altogether:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They are more precious than gold, yea, than much fine gold; and sweeter than honey and the dropping of the honeycomb.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Moreover, by them is thy servant enlightened; in keeping them there is great reward.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who understandeth [his] errors? Purify me from secret [faults].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Keep back thy servant also from presumptuous [sins]; let them not have dominion over me: then shall I be perfect, and I shall be innocent from great transgression.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let the words of my mouth and the meditation of my heart be acceptable in thy sight, O Jehovah, my rock, and my redeemer.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah answer thee in the day of trouble; the name of theGod of Jacob protect thee;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: May he send thee help from the sanctuary, and strengthen thee out of Zion;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Remember all thine oblations, and accept thy burnt-offering; Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Grant thee according to thy heart, and fulfil all thy counsels.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: We will triumph in thy salvation, and in the name of ourGod will we set up our banners. Jehovah fulfil all thy petitions!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now know I that Jehovah saveth his anointed; he answereth him from the heavens of his holiness, with the saving strength of his right hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Some make mention of chariots, and some of horses, but we of the name of Jehovah ourGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They are bowed down and fallen; but we are risen and stand upright.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Save, Jehovah! Let the king answer us in the day we call.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The king shall joy in thy strength, Jehovah; and in thy salvation how greatly shall he rejoice.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast given him his heart's desire, and hast not withholden the request of his lips. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thou hast met him with the blessings of goodness; thou hast set a crown of pure gold on his head.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He asked life of thee; thou gavest [it] him, length of days for ever and ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His glory is great through thy salvation; majesty and splendour hast thou laid upon him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thou hast made him to be blessings for ever; thou hast filled him with joy by thy countenance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the king confideth in Jehovah: and through the loving-kindness of the Most High he shall not be moved.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thy hand shall find out all thine enemies; thy right hand shall find out those that hate thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou shalt make them as a fiery furnace in the time of thy presence; Jehovah shall swallow them up in his anger, and the fire shall devour them:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Their fruit shalt thou destroy from the earth, and their seed from among the children of men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For they intended evil against thee; they imagined a mischievous device, which they could not execute.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thou wilt make them turn their back; thou wilt make ready thy bowstring against their face.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be thou exalted, Jehovah, in thine own strength: we will sing and celebrate thy power.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: MyGod, myGod, why hast thou forsaken me? [why art thou] far from my salvation, from the words of my groaning?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: MyGod, I cry by day, and thou answerest not; and by night, and there is no rest for me:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou art holy, thou that dwellest amid the praises of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Our fathers confided in thee: they confided, and thou didst deliver them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They cried unto thee, and were delivered; they confided in thee, and were not confounded.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I am a worm, and no man; a reproach of men, and the despised of the people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All they that see me laugh me to scorn; they shoot out the lip, they shake the head, [saying:]

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Commit it to Jehovah — let him rescue him; let him deliver him, because he delighteth in him!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But thou art he that took me out of the womb; thou didst make me trust, upon my mother's breasts.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I was cast upon thee from the womb; thou art myGod from my mother's belly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be not far from me, for trouble is near; for there is none to help.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Many bulls have encompassed me; Bashan's strong ones have beset me round.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They gape upon me with their mouth, [as] a ravening and a roaring lion.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I am poured out like water, and all my bones are out of joint: my heart is become like wax; it is melted in the midst of my bowels.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My strength is dried up like a potsherd, and my tongue cleaveth to my palate; and thou hast laid me in the dust of death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For dogs have encompassed me; an assembly of evil-doers have surrounded me: they pierced my hands and my feet.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I may count all my bones. They look, they stare upon me;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They part my garments among them, and cast lots upon my vesture.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But thou, Jehovah, be not far [from me]; O my strength, haste thee to help me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Deliver my soul from the sword; my only one from the power of the dog;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Save me from the lion's mouth. Yea, from the horns of the buffaloes hast thou answered me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will declare thy name unto my brethren, in the midst of the congregation will I praise thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye that fear Jehovah, praise him; all ye the seed of Jacob, glorify him; and revere him, all ye the seed of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he hath not despised nor abhorred the affliction of the afflicted; neither hath he hid his face from him: but when he cried unto him, he heard.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My praise is from thee, in the great congregation; I will pay my vows before them that fear him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The meek shall eat and be satisfied; they shall praise Jehovah that seek him: your heart shall live for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All the ends of the earth shall remember and turn unto Jehovah, and all the families of the nations shall worship before thee:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the kingdom is Jehovah's, and he ruleth among the nations.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All the fat ones of the earth shall eat and worship; all they that go down to the dust shall bow before him, and he that cannot keep alive his own soul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A seed shall serve him; it shall be accounted to the Lord for a generation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They shall come, and shall declare his righteousness unto a people that shall be born, that he hath done [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah is my shepherd; I shall not want.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He maketh me to lie down in green pastures; he leadeth me beside still waters.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He restoreth my soul; he leadeth me in paths of righteousness for his name's sake.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Yea, though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death, I will fear no evil: for thou art with me; thy rod and thy staff, they comfort me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou preparest a table before me in the presence of mine enemies; thou hast anointed my head with oil; my cup runneth over.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Surely, goodness and loving-kindness shall follow me all the days of my life; and I will dwell in the house of Jehovah for the length of the days.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The earth is Jehovah's, and the fulness thereof; the world, and they that dwell therein.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For it was he that founded it upon seas, and established it upon floods.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who shall ascend into the mount of Jehovah? and who shall stand in his holy place?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that hath blameless hands and a pure heart; who lifteth not up his soul unto vanity, nor sweareth deceitfully:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He shall receive blessing from Jehovah, and righteousness from theGod of his salvation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This is the generation of them that seek unto him, that seek thy face, O Jacob. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Lift up your heads, ye gates, and be ye lifted up, ye everlasting doors, and the King of glory shall come in.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who is this King of glory? Jehovah strong and mighty, Jehovah mighty in battle.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Lift up your heads, ye gates; yea, lift up, ye everlasting doors, and the King of glory shall come in.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who is he, this King of glory? Jehovah of hosts, he is the King of glory. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Unto thee, Jehovah, do I lift up my soul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: MyGod, I confide in thee; let me not be ashamed, let not mine enemies triumph over me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Yea, none that wait on thee shall be ashamed: they shall be ashamed that deal treacherously without cause.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Make me to know thy ways, O Jehovah; teach me thy paths.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Make me to walk in thy truth, and teach me: for thou art theGod of my salvation; on thee do I wait all the day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Remember, Jehovah, thy tender mercies and thy loving-kindnesses; for they are from everlasting.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Remember not the sins of my youth, nor my transgressions; according to thy loving-kindness remember thou me, for thy goodness' sake, Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Good and upright is Jehovah; therefore will he instruct sinners in the way:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The meek will he guide in judgment, and the meek will he teach his way.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All the paths of Jehovah are loving-kindness and truth for such as keep his covenant and his testimonies.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thy name's sake, O Jehovah, thou wilt indeed pardon mine iniquity; for it is great.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: What man is he that feareth Jehovah? him will he instruct in the way [that] he should choose.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His soul shall dwell in prosperity, and his seed shall inherit the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The secret of Jehovah is with them that fear him, that he may make known his covenant to them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Mine eyes are ever toward Jehovah; for he will bring my feet out of the net.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Turn toward me, and be gracious unto me; for I am solitary and afflicted.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The troubles of my heart are increased: bring me out of my distresses;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Consider mine affliction and my travail, and forgive all my sins.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Consider mine enemies, for they are many, and they hate me [with] cruel hatred.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Keep my soul, and deliver me: let me not be ashamed; for I trust in thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let integrity and uprightness preserve me; for I wait on thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Redeem Israel, OGod, out of all his troubles.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Judge me, O Jehovah, for I have walked in mine integrity, and I have confided in Jehovah: I shall not slip.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Prove me, Jehovah, and test me; try my reins and my heart:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thy loving-kindness is before mine eyes, and I have walked in thy truth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I have not sat with vain persons, neither have I gone in with dissemblers;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I have hated the congregation of evil-doers, and I have not sat with the wicked.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will wash my hands in innocency, and will encompass thine altar, O Jehovah,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: That I may cause the voice of thanksgiving to be heard, and declare all thy marvellous works.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah, I have loved the habitation of thy house, and the place where thy glory dwelleth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Gather not my soul with sinners, nor my life with men of blood;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In whose hands are evil devices, and their right hand is full of bribes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But as for me, I will walk in mine integrity. Redeem me, and be gracious unto me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My foot standeth in an even place; in the congregations will I bless Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah is my light and my salvation; whom shall I fear? Jehovah is the strength of my life; of whom shall I be afraid?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When evil-doers, mine adversaries and mine enemies, came upon me to eat up my flesh, they stumbled and fell.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If a host encamp against me, my heart shall not fear; if war rise against me, in this will I be confident.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: One [thing] have I asked of Jehovah, that will I seek after: that I may dwell in the house of Jehovah all the days of my life, to behold the beauty of Jehovah, and to inquire [of him] in his temple.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For in the day of evil he will hide me in his pavilion; in the secret of his tent will he keep me concealed: he will set me high upon a rock.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now shall my head be lifted up above mine enemies round about me; and I will offer in his tent sacrifices of shouts of joy: I will sing, yea, I will sing psalms unto Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hear, Jehovah; with my voice do I call; be gracious unto me, and answer me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My heart said for thee, Seek ye my face. Thy face, O Jehovah, will I seek.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hide not thy face from me; put not thy servant away in anger: thou hast been my help; cast me not off, neither forsake me, OGod of my salvation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For had my father and my mother forsaken me, then had Jehovah taken me up.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Teach me thy way, Jehovah, and lead me in an even path, because of mine enemies.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Deliver me not over to the will of mine adversaries; for false witnesses are risen up against me, and such as breathe out violence.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Unless I had believed to see the goodness of Jehovah in the land of the living...!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wait for Jehovah; be strong and let thy heart take courage: yea, wait for Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Unto thee, Jehovah, do I call; my rock, be not silent unto me, lest, [if] thou keep silence toward me, I become like them that go down into the pit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hear the voice of my supplications, when I cry unto thee, when I lift up my hands toward the oracle of thy holiness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Draw me not away with the wicked, and with the workers of iniquity, who speak peace to their neighbours, and mischief is in their heart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Give them according to their doing, and according to the wickedness of their deeds; give them after the work of their hands, render to them their desert.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For they regard not the deeds of Jehovah, nor the work of his hands: he will destroy them, and not build them up.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blessed be Jehovah, for he hath heard the voice of my supplications.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah is my strength and my shield; my heart confided in him, and I was helped: therefore my heart exulteth, and with my song will I praise him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah is their strength; and he is the stronghold of salvation to his anointed one.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Save thy people, and bless thine inheritance; and feed them, and lift them up for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Give unto Jehovah, ye sons of the mighty ones, give unto Jehovah glory and strength;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Give unto Jehovah the glory of his name; worship Jehovah in holy splendour.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The voice of Jehovah is upon the waters: theGod of glory thundereth, — Jehovah upon great waters.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The voice of Jehovah is powerful; the voice of Jehovah is full of majesty.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The voice of Jehovah breaketh cedars; yea, Jehovah breaketh the cedars of Lebanon:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he maketh them to skip like a calf, Lebanon and Sirion like a young buffalo.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The voice of Jehovah cleaveth out flames of fire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The voice of Jehovah shaketh the wilderness; Jehovah shaketh the wilderness of Kadesh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The voice of Jehovah maketh the hinds to calve, and layeth bare the forests; and in his temple doth every one say, Glory!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah sitteth upon the flood; yea, Jehovah sitteth as king for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah will give strength unto his people; Jehovah will bless his people with peace.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will extol thee, Jehovah; for thou hast delivered me, and hast not made mine enemies to rejoice over me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah myGod, I cried unto thee, and thou hast healed me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah, thou hast brought up my soul from Sheol, thou hast quickened me from among those that go down to the pit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Sing psalms unto Jehovah, ye saints of his, and give thanks in remembrance of his holiness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For a moment [is passed] in his anger, a life in his favour; at even weeping cometh for the night, and at morn there is rejoicing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As for me, I said in my prosperity, I shall never be moved.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah, by thy favour thou hadst made my mountain to stand strong: thou didst hide thy face; I was troubled.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I called to thee, Jehovah, and unto the Lord did I make supplication:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: What profit is there in my blood, in my going down to the pit? shall the dust praise thee? shall it declare thy truth?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hear, O Jehovah, and be gracious unto me; Jehovah, be my helper.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast turned for me my mourning into dancing; thou hast loosed my sackcloth, and girded me with gladness;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: That [my] glory may sing psalms of thee, and not be silent. Jehovah myGod, I will praise thee for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In thee, Jehovah, do I trust; let me never be ashamed: deliver me in thy righteousness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Incline thine ear to me, deliver me speedily; be a strong rock to me, a house of defence to save me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thou art my rock and my fortress; and, for thy name's sake, thou wilt lead me and guide me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Draw me out of the net that they have hidden for me; for thou art my strength.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Into thy hand I commit my spirit: thou hast redeemed me, Jehovah, [thou]God of truth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I have hated them that observe lying vanities; and as for me, I have confided in Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will be glad and rejoice in thy loving-kindness, for thou hast seen mine affliction; thou hast known the troubles of my soul,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And hast not shut me up into the hand of the enemy: thou hast set my feet in a large place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be gracious unto me, Jehovah, for I am in trouble: mine eye wasteth away with vexation, my soul and my belly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For my life is spent with sorrow, and my years with sighing; my strength faileth through mine iniquity, and my bones are wasted.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: More than to all mine oppressors, I am become exceedingly a reproach, even to my neighbours, and a fear to mine acquaintance: they that see me without flee from me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I am forgotten in [their] heart as a dead man; I am become like a broken vessel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I have heard the slander of many — terror on every side — when they take counsel together against me: they plot to take away my life.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I confided in thee, Jehovah; I said, thou art myGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My times are in thy hand: deliver me from the hand of mine enemies, and from my persecutors.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Make thy face to shine upon thy servant; save me in thy loving-kindness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah, let me not be ashamed; for I have called upon thee: let the wicked be ashamed, let them be silent in Sheol.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let the lying lips become dumb, which speak insolently against the righteous in pride and contempt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [Oh] how great is thy goodness, which thou hast laid up for them that fear thee, [which] thou hast wrought for them that trust in thee, before the sons of men!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou keepest them concealed in the secret of thy presence from the conspiracies of man; thou hidest them in a pavilion from the strife of tongues.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blessed be Jehovah; for he hath shewn me wondrously his loving-kindness in a strong city.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As for me, I said in my haste, I am cut off from before thine eyes; nevertheless thou heardest the voice of my supplications when I cried unto thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Love Jehovah, all ye his saints. Jehovah preserveth the faithful, and plentifully requiteth the proud doer.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be strong, and let your heart take courage, all ye that hope in Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blessed is he [whose] transgression is forgiven, [whose] sin is covered!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blessed is the man unto whom Jehovah reckoneth not iniquity, and in whose spirit there is no guile!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When I kept silence, my bones waxed old, through my groaning all the day long.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For day and night thy hand was heavy upon me; my moisture was turned into the drought of summer. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I acknowledged my sin unto thee, and mine iniquity I covered not; I said, I will confess my transgressions unto Jehovah, and thou forgavest the iniquity of my sin. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For this shall every one that isgodly pray unto thee at a time when thou mayest be found: surely in the floods of great waters they will not reach him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou art a hiding-place for me; thou preservest me from trouble; thou dost encompass me with songs of deliverance. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will instruct thee and teach thee the way in which thou shalt go; I will counsel [thee] with mine eye upon thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be ye not as a horse, as a mule, which have no understanding: whose trappings must be bit and bridle, for restraint, or they will not come unto thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Many sorrows hath the wicked; but he that confideth in Jehovah, loving-kindness shall encompass him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Rejoice in Jehovah, and be glad, ye righteous; and shout for joy, all ye upright in heart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Exult, ye righteous, in Jehovah: praise is comely for the upright.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Give thanks unto Jehovah with the harp; sing psalms unto him with the ten-stringed lute.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Sing unto him a new song; play skilfully with a loud sound.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the word of Jehovah is right, and all his work is in faithfulness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He loveth righteousness and judgment: the earth is full of the loving-kindness of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: By the word of Jehovah were the heavens made, and all the host of them by the breath of his mouth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He gathereth the waters of the sea together as a heap; he layeth up the deeps in storehouses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let all the earth fear Jehovah; let all the inhabitants of the world stand in awe of him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he spoke, and it was [done]; he commanded, and it stood fast.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah frustrateth the counsel of the nations; he maketh the thoughts of the peoples of none effect.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The counsel of Jehovah standeth for ever, the thoughts of his heart from generation to generation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blessed is the nation whoseGod is Jehovah, the people that he hath chosen for his inheritance!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah looketh from the heavens; he beholdeth all the sons of men:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: From the place of his habitation he looketh forth upon all the inhabitants of the earth;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He who fashioneth the hearts of them all, who considereth all their works.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The king is not saved by the multitude of [his] forces; a mighty man is not delivered by much strength.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The horse is a vain thing for safety; neither doth he deliver by his great power.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, the eye of Jehovah is upon them that fear him, upon them that hope in his loving-kindness,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: To deliver their soul from death, and to keep them alive in famine.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Our soul waiteth for Jehovah: he is our help and our shield.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For in him shall our heart rejoice, because we have confided in his holy name.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let thy loving-kindness, O Jehovah, be upon us, according as we have hoped in thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will bless Jehovah at all times; his praise shall continually be in my mouth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My soul shall make its boast in Jehovah: the meek shall hear, and rejoice.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Magnify Jehovah with me, and let us exalt his name together.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I sought Jehovah, and he answered me, and delivered me from all my fears.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They looked unto him, and were enlightened, and their faces were not confounded.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This afflicted one called, and Jehovah heard [him], and saved him out of all his troubles.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The angel of Jehovah encampeth round about them that fear him, and delivereth them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Taste and see that Jehovah is good: blessed is the man that trusteth in him!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Fear Jehovah, ye his saints; for there is no want to them that fear him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The young lions are in need and suffer hunger; but they that seek Jehovah shall not want any good.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Come, ye sons, hearken unto me: I will teach you the fear of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: What man is he that desireth life, [and] loveth days, that he may see good?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Keep thy tongue from evil, and thy lips from speaking guile;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Depart from evil, and do good; seek peace, and pursue it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The eyes of Jehovah are upon the righteous, and his ears are toward their cry;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The face of Jehovah is against them that do evil, to cut off the remembrance of them from the earth:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [The righteous] cry, and Jehovah heareth, and delivereth them out of all their troubles.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah is nigh to those that are of a broken heart, and saveth them that are of a contrite spirit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Many are the adversities of the righteous, but Jehovah delivereth him out of them all:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He keepeth all his bones; not one of them is broken.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Evil shall destroy the wicked; and they that hate the righteous shall bear their guilt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah redeemeth the soul of his servants; and none of them that trust in him shall bear guilt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Strive, O Jehovah, with them that strive with me; fight against them that fight against me:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Take hold of shield and buckler, and stand up for my help;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And draw out the spear, and stop [the way] against my pursuers: say unto my soul, I am thy salvation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let them be put to shame and confounded that seek after my life; let them be turned backward and brought to confusion that devise my hurt:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let them be as chaff before the wind, and let the angel of Jehovah drive [them] away;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let their way be dark and slippery, and let the angel of Jehovah pursue them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For without cause have they hidden for me their net [in] a pit; without cause they have digged [it] for my soul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let destruction come upon him unawares, and let his net which he hath hidden catch himself: for destruction let him fall therein.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And my soul shall be joyful in Jehovah; it shall rejoice in his salvation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All my bones shall say, Jehovah, who is like unto thee, who deliverest the afflicted from one stronger than he, yea, the afflicted and the needy from him that spoileth him!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Unrighteous witnesses rise up; they lay to my charge things which I know not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They reward me evil for good, [to] the bereavement of my soul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But as for me, when they were sick, my clothing was sackcloth; I chastened my soul with fasting, and my prayer returned into mine own bosom:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I behaved myself as though [he had been] a friend, a brother to me; I bowed down in sadness, as one that mourneth [for] a mother.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But at my halting they rejoiced, and gathered together: the slanderers gathered themselves together against me, and I knew [it] not; they did tear [me], and ceased not:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: With profane jesters for bread, they have gnashed their teeth against me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Lord, how long wilt thou look on? Rescue my soul from their destructions, my only one from the young lions.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will give thee thanks in the great congregation; I will praise thee among much people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let not them that are wrongfully mine enemies rejoice over me; let them not wink with the eye that hate me without cause.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For they speak not peace; and they devise deceitful words against the quiet in the land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they opened their mouth wide against me; they said, Aha! aha! our eye hath seen [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast seen [it], Jehovah: keep not silence; O Lord, be not far from me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Stir up thyself, and awake for my right, for my cause, myGod and Lord!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Judge me, Jehovah myGod, according to thy righteousness, and let them not rejoice over me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let them not say in their heart, Aha! so would we have it. Let them not say, We have swallowed him up.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let them be ashamed and brought to confusion together that rejoice at mine adversity; let them be clothed with shame and dishonour that magnify themselves against me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let them exult and rejoice that delight in my righteousness; and let them say continually, Jehovah be magnified, who delighteth in the prosperity of his servant.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And my tongue shall talk of thy righteousness, [and] of thy praise, all the day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The transgression of the wicked uttereth within my heart, There is no fear ofGod before his eyes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he flattereth himself in his own eyes, [even] when his iniquity is found to be hateful.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The words of his mouth are wickedness and deceit: he hath left off to be wise, to do good.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He deviseth wickedness up on his bed; he setteth himself in a way that is not good: he abhorreth not evil.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah, thy loving-kindness is in the heavens, and thy faithfulness [reacheth] unto the clouds.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thy righteousness is like the high mountains; thy judgments are a great deep: thou, Jehovah, preservest man and beast.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How precious is thy loving-kindness, OGod! So the sons of men take refuge under the shadow of thy wings.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They shall be abundantly satisfied with the fatness of thy house; and thou wilt make them drink of the river of thy pleasures.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For with thee is the fountain of life: in thy light shall we see light.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Continue thy loving-kindness unto them that know thee, and thy righteousness to the upright in heart;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let not the foot of pride come against me, and let not the hand of the wicked drive me away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There are the workers of iniquity fallen: they are cast down, and are not able to rise.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Fret not thyself because of evil-doers, and be not envious of them that work unrighteousness;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for they shall soon be cut down like the grass, and fade as the green herb.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Confide in Jehovah, and do good; dwell in the land, and feed on faithfulness;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and delight thyself in Jehovah, and he will give thee the desires of thy heart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Commit thy way unto Jehovah, and rely upon him: he will bring [it] to pass;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he will bring forth thy righteousness as the light, and thy judgment as the noonday.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Rest in Jehovah, and wait patiently for him: fret not thyself because of him that prospereth in his way, because of the man that bringeth mischievous devices to pass.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Cease from anger, and forsake wrath; fret not thyself: it [would be] only to do evil.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For evil-doers shall be cut off; but those that wait on Jehovah, they shall possess the land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For yet a little while, and the wicked is not; and thou considerest his place, but he is not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the meek shall possess the land, and shall delight themselves in the abundance of prosperity.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The wicked plotteth against the righteous, and gnasheth his teeth against him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The Lord laugheth at him; for he seeth that his day is coming.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The wicked have drawn out the sword, and have bent their bow, to cast down the afflicted and needy, to slay those that are upright in [the] way:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: their sword shall enter into their own heart, and their bows shall be broken.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The little that the righteous hath is better than the abundance of many wicked;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for the arms of the wicked shall be broken, but Jehovah upholdeth the righteous.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah knoweth the days of the perfect; and their inheritance shall be for ever:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: they shall not be ashamed in the time of evil, and in the days of famine they shall be satisfied.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the wicked shall perish, and the enemies of Jehovah shall be as the fat of lambs: they shall consume, like smoke shall they consume away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The wicked borroweth, and payeth not again; but the righteous is gracious and giveth:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for those blessed of him shall possess the land, and they that are cursed of him shall be cut off.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The steps of a man are established by Jehovah, and he delighteth in his way:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: though he fall, he shall not be utterly cast down, for Jehovah upholdeth his hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I have been young, and now am old, and I have not seen the righteous forsaken, nor his seed seeking bread:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: all the day he is gracious and lendeth, and his seed shall be a blessing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Depart from evil, and do good, and dwell for evermore;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for Jehovah loveth judgment, and will not forsake his saints: They are preserved for ever; but the seed of the wicked shall be cut off.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The righteous shall possess the land, and dwell therein for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The mouth of the righteous proffereth wisdom, and his tongue speaketh judgment;

translations.name: Darby Bible
