translations.text: the law of hisGod is in his heart; his goings shall not slide.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The wicked watcheth the righteous, and seeketh to slay him:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah will not leave him in his hand, nor condemn him when he is judged.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wait for Jehovah, and keep his way, and he will exalt thee to possess the land: when the wicked are cut off, thou shalt see [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I have seen the wicked in great power, and spreading like a green tree in its native soil:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but he passed away, and behold, he was not; and I sought him, but he was not found.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Mark the perfect, and behold the upright, for the end of [that] man is peace;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but the transgressors shall be destroyed together; the future of the wicked shall be cut off.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the salvation of the righteous is of Jehovah: he is their strength in the time of trouble.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah will help them and deliver them: he will deliver them from the wicked, and save them; for they trust in him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah, rebuke me not in thy wrath; neither chasten me in thy hot displeasure.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thine arrows stick fast in me, and thy hand cometh down upon me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There is no soundness in my flesh because of thine indignation; no peace in my bones, because of my sin.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For mine iniquities are gone over my head: as a heavy burden they are too heavy for me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My wounds stink, they are corrupt, because of my foolishness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I am depressed; I am bowed down beyond measure; I go mourning all the day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For my loins are full of burning, and there is no soundness in my flesh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I am faint and broken beyond measure; I roar by reason of the agitation of my heart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Lord, all my desire is before thee, and my sighing is not hid from thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My heart throbbeth, my strength hath left me; and the light of mine eyes, it also is no more with me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My lovers and mine associates stand aloof from my stroke; and my kinsmen stand afar off.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they that seek after my life lay snares [for me]; and they that seek my hurt speak mischievous things, and meditate deceits all the day long.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I, as a deaf [man], hear not; and am as a dumb man that openeth not his mouth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Yea, I am as a man that heareth not, and in whose mouth are no reproofs.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For in thee, Jehovah, do I hope: thou wilt answer, O Lord myGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I said, Let them not rejoice over me! When my foot slipped, they magnified [themselves] against me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I am ready to halt, and my pain is continually before me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I will declare mine iniquity, I am grieved for my sin.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But mine enemies are lively, they are strong; and they that hate me wrongfully are multiplied:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they that render evil for good are adversaries unto me; because I pursue what is good.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Forsake me not, Jehovah; O myGod, be not far from me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Make haste to help me, O Lord, my salvation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I said, I will take heed to my ways, that I sin not with my tongue: I will keep my mouth with a muzzle, while the wicked is before me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I was dumb with silence, I held my peace from good; and my sorrow was stirred.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My heart burned within me; the fire was kindled in my musing: I spoke with my tongue,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Make me to know, Jehovah, mine end, and the measure of my days, what it is: I shall know how frail I am.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, thou hast made my days [as] hand-breadths, and my lifetime is as nothing before thee; verily, every man, [even] the high placed, is altogether vanity. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Verily, man walketh in a vain show; verily they are disquieted in vain; he heapeth up [riches], and knoweth not who shall gather them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, what wait I for, Lord? my hope is in thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Deliver me from all my transgressions; make me not the reproach of the foolish.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I was dumb, I opened not my mouth; for thou hast done [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Remove thy stroke away from me: I am consumed by the blow of thy hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When thou with rebukes dost correct a man for iniquity, thou makest his beauty to consume away like a moth: surely, every man is vanity. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hear my prayer, Jehovah, and give ear unto my cry; be not silent at my tears: for I am a stranger with thee, a sojourner, like all my fathers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Look away from me, and let me recover strength, before I go hence and be no more.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I waited patiently for Jehovah; and he inclined unto me, and heard my cry.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he brought me up out of the pit of destruction, out of the miry clay, and set my feet upon a rock; he hath established my goings:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he hath put a new song in my mouth, praise unto ourGod. Many shall see it, and fear, and shall confide in Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blessed is the man that hath made Jehovah his confidence, and turneth not to the proud, and to such as turn aside to lies.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou, O Jehovah myGod, hast multiplied thy marvellous works, and thy thoughts toward us: they cannot be reckoned up in order unto thee; would I declare and speak [them], they are more than can be numbered.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Sacrifice and oblation thou didst not desire: ears hast thou prepared me. Burnt-offering and sin-offering hast thou not demanded;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then said I, Behold, I come, in the volume of the book it is written of me —

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: To do thy good pleasure, myGod, is my delight, and thy law is within my heart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I have published righteousness in the great congregation: behold, I have not withheld my lips, Jehovah, thou knowest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I have not hidden thy righteousness within my heart; I have declared thy faithfulness and thy salvation: I have not concealed thy loving-kindness and thy truth from the great congregation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Withhold not thou, Jehovah, thy tender mercies from me; let thy loving-kindness and thy truth continually preserve me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For innumerable evils have compassed me about: mine iniquities have taken hold upon me, so that I cannot see; they are more than the hairs of my head: and my heart hath failed me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be pleased, O Jehovah, to deliver me; Jehovah, make haste to my help.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let them be ashamed and brought to confusion together that seek after my soul to destroy it; let them be turned backward and confounded that take pleasure in mine adversity;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let them be desolate, because of their shame, that say unto me, Aha! Aha!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let all those that seek thee be glad and rejoice in thee; let such as love thy salvation say continually, Jehovah be magnified!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I am afflicted and needy: the Lord thinketh upon me. Thou art my help and my deliverer: myGod, make no delay.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blessed is he that understandeth the poor: Jehovah will deliver him in the day of evil.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah will preserve him, and keep him alive; he shall be made happy in the land; and thou wilt not deliver him to the will of his enemies.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah will sustain him upon the bed of languishing: thou turnest all his bed in his sickness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As for me, I said, Jehovah, be gracious unto me: heal my soul; for I have sinned against thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Mine enemies wish me evil: When will he die, and his name perish?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if one come to see [me], he speaketh falsehood; his heart gathereth wickedness to itself: he goeth abroad, he telleth [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All that hate me whisper together against me; against me do they devise my hurt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A thing of Belial cleaveth fast unto him; and now that he is laid down, he will rise up no more.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Yea, mine own familiar friend, in whom I confided, who did eat of my bread, hath lifted up [his] heel against me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But thou, Jehovah, be gracious unto me, and raise me up, that I may requite them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: By this I know that thou delightest in me, because mine enemy doth not triumph over me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But as for me, thou upholdest me in mine integrity, and settest me before thy face for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blessed be Jehovah, theGod of Israel, from eternity to eternity! Amen, and Amen.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As the hart panteth after the water-brooks, so panteth my soul after thee, OGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My soul thirsteth forGod, for the livingGod: when shall I come and appear beforeGod?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My tears have been my bread day and night, while they say unto me all the day, Where is thyGod?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These things I remember and have poured out my soul within me: how I passed along with the multitude, how I went on with them to the house ofGod, with the voice of joy and praise, a festive multitude.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Why art thou cast down, my soul, and art disquieted in me? hope inGod; for I shall yet praise him, [for] the health of his countenance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: MyGod, my soul is cast down within me; therefore do I remember thee from the land of the Jordan, and the Hermons, from mount Mizar.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Deep calleth unto deep at the noise of thy cataracts; all thy breakers and thy billows are gone over me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In the day-time will Jehovah command his loving-kindness, and in the night his song shall be with me, a prayer unto theGod of my life.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will say untoGod my rock, Why hast thou forgotten me? why go I mourning because of the oppression of the enemy?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As with a crushing in my bones mine adversaries reproach me, while they say unto me all the day, Where is thyGod?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Why art thou cast down, my soul? and why art thou disquieted within me? hope inGod; for I shall yet praise him, [who is] the health of my countenance, and myGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Judge me, OGod, and plead my cause against an ungodly nation; deliver me from the deceitful and unrighteous man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thou art theGod of my strength: why hast thou cast me off? why go I about mourning because of the oppression of the enemy?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Send out thy light and thy truth: they shall lead me, they shall bring me to thy holy mount, and unto thy habitations.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then will I go unto the altar ofGod, unto theGod of the gladness of my joy: yea, upon the harp will I praise thee, OGod, myGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Why art thou cast down, my soul? and why art thou disquieted within me? hope inGod; for I shall yet praise him, [who is] the health of my countenance, and myGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: OGod, with our ears have we heard, our fathers have told us, the work thou wroughtest in their days, in the days of old:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou, by thy hand, didst dispossess the nations, but them thou didst plant; thou didst afflict the peoples, but them didst thou cause to spread out.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For not by their own sword did they take possession of the land, neither did their own arm save them; but thy right hand, and thine arm, and the light of thy countenance, because thou hadst delight in them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou thyself art my king, OGod: command deliverance for Jacob.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Through thee will we push down our adversaries; through thy name will we tread them under that rise up against us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I will not put confidence in my bow, neither shall my sword save me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thou hast saved us from our adversaries, and hast put them to shame that hate us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: InGod will we boast all the day, and we will praise thy name for ever. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But thou hast cast off, and put us to confusion, and dost not go forth with our armies;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast made us to turn back from the adversary, and they that hate us spoil for themselves;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast given us over like sheep [appointed] for meat, and hast scattered us among the nations;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast sold thy people for nought, and hast not increased [thy wealth] by their price;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou makest us a reproach to our neighbours, a mockery and a derision for them that are round about us;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou makest us a byword among the nations, a shaking of the head among the peoples.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All the day my confusion is before me, and the shame of my face hath covered me,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Because of the voice of him that reproacheth and blasphemeth; by reason of the enemy and the avenger.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All this is come upon us; yet have we not forgotten thee, neither have we dealt falsely against thy covenant:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Our heart is not turned back, neither have our steps declined from thy path;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Though thou hast crushed us in the place of jackals, and covered us with the shadow of death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If we had forgotten the name of ourGod, and stretched out our hands to a strangegod,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Would notGod search this out? for he knoweth the secrets of the heart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But for thy sake are we killed all the day long; we are reckoned as sheep for slaughter.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Awake, why sleepest thou, Lord? arise, cast [us] not off for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wherefore hidest thou thy face, [and] forgettest our affliction and our oppression?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For our soul is bowed down to the dust; our belly cleaveth unto the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Rise up for our help, and redeem us for thy loving-kindness' sake.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My heart is welling forth [with] a good matter: I say what I have composed touching the king. My tongue is the pen of a ready writer.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou art fairer than the sons of men; grace is poured into thy lips: thereforeGod hath blessed thee for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Gird thy sword upon [thy] thigh, O mighty one, [in] thy majesty and thy splendour;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And [in] thy splendour ride prosperously, because of truth and meekness [and] righteousness: and thy right hand shall teach thee terrible things.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thine arrows are sharp — peoples fall under thee — in the heart of the king's enemies.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thy throne, OGod, is for ever and ever; a sceptre of uprightness is the sceptre of thy kingdom:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast loved righteousness, and hated wickedness; thereforeGod, thyGod, hath anointed thee with the oil of gladness above thy companions.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Myrrh and aloes, cassia, are all thy garments; out of ivory palaces stringed instruments have made thee glad.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Kings' daughters are among thine honourable women; upon thy right hand doth stand the queen in gold of Ophir.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hearken, daughter, and see, and incline thine ear; and forget thine own people and thy father's house:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king will desire thy beauty; for he is thy Lord, and worship thou him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the daughter of Tyre with a gift, the rich ones among the people, shall court thy favour.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All glorious is the king's daughter within; her clothing is of wrought gold:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: She shall be brought unto the king in raiment of embroidery; the virgins behind her, her companions, shall be brought in unto thee:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: With joy and gladness shall they be brought; they shall enter into the king's palace.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Instead of thy fathers shall be thy sons; princes shalt thou make them in all the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will make thy name to be remembered throughout all generations; therefore shall the peoples praise thee for ever and ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: God is our refuge and strength, a help in distresses, very readily found.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore will we not fear though the earth be removed, and though the mountains be carried into the heart of the seas;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Though the waters thereof roar [and] foam, though the mountains shake with the swelling thereof. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There is a river the streams whereof make glad the city ofGod, the sanctuary of the habitations of the Most High.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: God is in the midst of her; she shall not be moved:God shall help her at the dawn of the morning.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The nations raged, the kingdoms were moved; he uttered his voice, the earth melted.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah of hosts is with us; theGod of Jacob is our high fortress. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Come, behold the works of Jehovah, what desolations he hath made in the earth:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He hath made wars to cease unto the end of the earth; he breaketh the bow, and cutteth the spear in sunder; he burneth the chariots in the fire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be still, and know that I amGod: I will be exalted among the nations, I will be exalted in the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah of hosts is with us; theGod of Jacob is our high fortress. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All ye peoples, clap your hands; shout untoGod with the voice of triumph!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For Jehovah, the Most High, is terrible, a great king over all the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He subdueth the peoples under us, and the nations under our feet.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He hath chosen our inheritance for us, the excellency of Jacob whom he loved. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: God is gone up amid shouting, Jehovah amid the sound of the trumpet.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Sing psalms ofGod, sing psalms; sing psalms unto our King, sing psalms!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: ForGod is the King of all the earth; sing psalms with understanding.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: God reigneth over the nations;God sitteth upon the throne of his holiness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The willing-hearted of the peoples have gathered together, [with] the people of theGod of Abraham. For untoGod [belong] the shields of the earth: he is greatly exalted.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Great is Jehovah, and greatly to be praised in the city of ourGod, in the hill of his holiness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Beautiful in elevation, the joy of the whole earth, is mount Zion, [on] the sides of the north, the city of the great King.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: God is known in her palaces as a high fortress.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For behold, the kings assembled themselves, they passed by together;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They saw, — so they marvelled; they were troubled, they fled in consternation:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Trembling took hold upon them there; anguish, as of a woman in travail.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: With an east wind thou hast broken the ships of Tarshish.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As we have heard, so have we seen, in the city of Jehovah of hosts, in the city of ourGod:God doth establish it for ever. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: We have thought, OGod, of thy loving-kindness, in the midst of thy temple.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: According to thy name, OGod, so is thy praise unto the ends of the earth: thy right hand is full of righteousness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let mount Zion rejoice, let the daughters of Judah be glad, because of thy judgments.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Walk about Zion, and go round about her: count the towers thereof;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Mark ye well her bulwarks, consider her palaces: that ye may tell it to the generation following.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thisGod is ourGod for ever and ever; he will be our guide until death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hear this, all ye peoples; give ear, all inhabitants of the world:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Both men of low and men of high degree, rich and poor alike.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My mouth shall speak wisdom, and the meditation of my heart shall be of understanding:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will incline mine ear to a parable, I will open my riddle upon the harp.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wherefore should I fear in the days of adversity, [when] the iniquity of my supplanters encompasseth me? —

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They depend upon their wealth, and boast themselves in the abundance of their riches. ...

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: None can by any means redeem his brother, nor give toGod a ransom for him,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (For the redemption of their soul is costly, and must be given up for ever,)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: That he should still live perpetually, [and] not see corruption.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he seeth that wise men die; all alike, the fool and the brutish perish, and they leave their wealth to others.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Their inward thought is, that their houses are for ever, their dwelling-places from generation to generation: they call the lands after their own names.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Nevertheless, man being in honour abideth not: he is like the beasts that perish.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This their way is their folly, yet they that come after them delight in their sayings. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Like sheep are they laid in Sheol: Death feedeth on them; and the upright shall have dominion over them in the morning; and their comeliness shall be for Sheol to consume, that there be no habitation for them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: ButGod will redeem my soul from the power of Sheol: for he will receive me. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be not afraid when a man becometh rich, when the glory of his house is increased:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For when he dieth, he shall carry nothing away; his glory shall not descend after him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Though he blessed his soul in his lifetime, — and men will praise thee when thou doest well to thyself, —

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It shall go to the generation of his fathers: they shall never see light.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Man that is in honour, and understandeth not, is like the beasts that perish.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: God, Elohim-Jehovah, hath spoken, and called the earth from the rising of the sun unto the going down thereof.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Out of Zion, the perfection of beauty,God hath shined forth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: OurGod will come, and will not keep silence: fire shall devour before him, and it shall be very tempestuous round about him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He will call to the heavens from above, and to the earth, that he may judge his people:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Gather unto me mygodly ones, those that have made a covenant with me by sacrifice!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the heavens shall declare his righteousness; forGod executeth judgment himself. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hear, my people, and I will speak; O Israel, and I will testify unto thee: I amGod, thyGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will not reprove thee for thy sacrifices, or thy burnt-offerings, continually before me;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will take no bullock out of thy house, [nor] he-goats out of thy folds:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For every beast of the forest is mine, the cattle upon a thousand hills;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I know all the fowl of the mountains, and the roaming creatures of the field are mine:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If I were hungry, I would not tell thee; for the world is mine, and the fulness thereof.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Should I eat the flesh of bulls, and drink the blood of goats?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Offer untoGod thanksgiving, and perform thy vows unto the Most High;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And call upon me in the day of trouble; I will deliver thee, and thou shalt glorify me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But unto the wickedGod saith, What hast thou to do to declare my statutes, or that thou shouldest take my covenant into thy mouth,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Seeing thou hast hated correction and hast cast my words behind thee?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When thou sawest a thief, thou didst take pleasure in him, and thy portion was with adulterers;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou lettest thy mouth loose to evil, and thy tongue frameth deceit;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou sittest [and] speakest against thy brother, thou revilest thine own mother's son:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These [things] hast thou done, and I kept silence; thou thoughtest that I was altogether as thyself: [but] I will reprove thee, and set [them] in order before thine eyes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now consider this, ye that forgetGod, lest I tear in pieces, and there be no deliverer.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whoso offereth praise glorifieth me; and to him that ordereth [his] way will I shew the salvation ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be gracious unto me, OGod, according to thy loving-kindness; according to the abundance of thy tender mercies, blot out my transgressions.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wash me fully from mine iniquity, and cleanse me from my sin.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I acknowledge my transgressions, and my sin is continually before me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Against thee, thee only, have I sinned, and done what is evil in thy sight; that thou mayest be justified when thou speakest, be clear when thou judgest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, in iniquity was I brought forth, and in sin did my mother conceive me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, thou wilt have truth in the inward parts; and in the hidden [part] thou wilt make me to know wisdom.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Purge me with hyssop, and I shall be clean; wash me, and I shall be whiter than snow.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Make me to hear gladness and joy; [that] the bones which thou hast broken may rejoice.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hide thy face from my sins, and blot out all mine iniquities.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Create in me a clean heart, OGod, and renew a steadfast spirit within me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Cast me not away from thy presence, and take not the spirit of thy holiness from me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Restore unto me the joy of thy salvation, and let a willing spirit sustain me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will teach transgressors thy ways, and sinners shall return unto thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Deliver me from blood-guiltiness, OGod, thouGod of my salvation: my tongue shall sing aloud of thy righteousness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Lord, open my lips, and my mouth shall declare thy praise.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thou desirest not sacrifice; else would I give it: thou hast no pleasure in burnt-offering.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The sacrifices ofGod are a broken spirit: a broken and a contrite heart, OGod, thou wilt not despise.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Do good in thy good pleasure unto Zion; build the walls of Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then shalt thou have sacrifices of righteousness, burnt-offering, and whole burnt-offering; then shall they offer up bullocks upon thine altar.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Why boastest thou thyself in evil, thou mighty man? The loving-kindness ofGod [abideth] continually.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thy tongue deviseth mischievous things, like a sharp razor, practising deceit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast loved evil rather than good, lying rather than to speak righteousness. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast loved all devouring words, O deceitful tongue!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: God shall likewise destroy thee for ever; he shall take thee away, and pluck thee out of [thy] tent, and root thee out of the land of the living. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The righteous also shall see, and fear, and shall laugh at him, [saying,]

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold the man that made notGod his strength, but put confidence in the abundance of his riches, [and] strengthened himself in his avarice.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But as for me, I am like a green olive-tree in the house ofGod: I will confide in the loving-kindness ofGod for ever and ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will praise thee for ever, because thou hast done [it]; and I will wait on thy name, before thygodly ones, for it is good.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The fool hath said in his heart, There is noGod! They have corrupted themselves, and have done abominable iniquity: there is none that doeth good.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: God looked down from the heavens upon the children of men, to see if there were any that did understand, that did seekGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Every one of them is gone back, they are together become corrupt: there is none that doeth good, not even one.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Have the workers of iniquity no knowledge, eating up my people [as] they eat bread? they call not uponGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There were they in great fear, where no fear was; forGod scattereth the bones of him that encampeth against thee. Thou hast put [them] to shame, forGod hath despised them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Oh that the salvation of Israel were come out of Zion! WhenGod turneth again the captivity of his people, Jacob shall be glad, Israel shall rejoice.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: OGod, by thy name save me, and by thy strength do me justice.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: OGod, hear my prayer; give ear to the words of my mouth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For strangers are risen up against me, and the violent seek after my life: they have not setGod before them. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold,God is my helper; the Lord is among them that uphold my soul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He will requite evil to mine enemies: in thy truth cut them off.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will freely sacrifice unto thee; I will praise thy name, O Jehovah, because it is good.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he hath delivered me out of all trouble; and mine eye hath seen [its desire] upon mine enemies.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Give ear to my prayer, OGod; and hide not thyself from my supplication.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Attend unto me, and answer me: I wander about in my plaint, and I moan aloud,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Because of the voice of the enemy; because of the oppression of the wicked: for they cast iniquity upon me, and in anger they persecute me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My heart is writhing within me, and the terrors of death are fallen upon me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Fear and trembling are come upon me, and horror hath overwhelmed me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I said, Oh that I had wings like a dove! I would fly away, and be at rest;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, I would flee afar off, I would lodge in the wilderness; Selah;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I would hasten my escape from the stormy wind, from the tempest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Swallow [them] up, Lord; divide their tongue: for I have seen violence and strife in the city.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Day and night they go about it upon the walls thereof; and iniquity and mischief are in the midst of it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Perversities are in the midst thereof; and oppression and deceit depart not from its streets.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For it is not an enemy that hath reproached me — then could I have borne it; neither is it he that hateth me that hath magnified [himself] against me — then would I have hidden myself from him;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But it was thou, a man mine equal, mine intimate, my familiar friend. ...

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: We who held sweet intercourse together. To the house ofGod we walked amid the throng.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let death seize upon them, let them go down alive into Sheol. For wickedness is in their dwellings, in their midst.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As for me, untoGod will I call; and Jehovah will save me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Evening, and morning, and at noon, will I pray and moan aloud; and he will hear my voice.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He hath redeemed my soul in peace from the battle against me: for there were many about me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: God will hear, and afflict them: he that is seated of old, (Selah) ...because there is no change in them, and they fear notGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He hath put forth his hands against such as are at peace with him; he hath profaned his covenant.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Smooth were the milky [words] of his mouth, but his heart was war; his words were softer than oil, yet are they drawn swords.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Cast thy burden upon Jehovah, and he will sustain thee: he will never suffer the righteous to be moved.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou, OGod, wilt bring them down into the pit of destruction: bloody and deceitful men shall not live out half their days. But as for me, I will confide in thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be gracious unto me, OGod; for man would swallow me up: all the day long fighting he oppresseth me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Mine enemies would swallow [me] up all the day long; for they are many that fight against me haughtily.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In the day that I am afraid, I will confide in thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: InGod will I praise his word, inGod I put my confidence: I will not fear; what can flesh do unto me?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All the day long they wrest my words; all their thoughts are against me for evil.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They gather themselves together, they hide themselves; they mark my steps, because they wait for my soul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Shall they escape by iniquity? In anger cast down the peoples, OGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou countest my wanderings; put my tears into thy bottle: are they not in thy book?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then shall mine enemies return backward in the day when I call: this I know, forGod is for me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: InGod will I praise [his] word; in Jehovah will I praise [his] word.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: InGod have I put my confidence: I will not fear; what can man do unto me?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thy vows are upon me, OGod: I will render thanks unto thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thou hast delivered my soul from death; [wilt thou] not [keep] my feet from falling, that I may walk beforeGod in the light of the living?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be gracious unto me, OGod, be gracious unto me; for my soul taketh refuge in thee: yea, in the shadow of thy wings do I take refuge, until the calamities be overpast.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will call untoGod, the Most High; untoGod that performeth [all] for me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He will send from the heavens and save me; he hath covered with reproach him that would swallow me up. Selah.God hath sent forth his loving-kindness and his truth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My soul is in the midst of lions; I lie down [among] them that breathe out flames, the sons of men, whose teeth are spears and arrows, and their tongue a sharp sword.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be exalted above the heavens, OGod; let thy glory be above all the earth!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They have prepared a net for my steps; my soul was bowed down: they have digged a pit before me; they are fallen into the midst thereof. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My heart is fixed, OGod, my heart is fixed: I will sing, yea, I will sing psalms.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Awake, my glory; awake, lute and harp: I will wake the dawn.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will give thee thanks among the peoples, O Lord; of thee will I sing psalms among the nations:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thy loving-kindness is great unto the heavens, and thy truth unto the clouds.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be exalted above the heavens, OGod; let thy glory be above all the earth!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Is righteousness indeed silent? Do ye speak it? Do ye judge with equity, ye sons of men?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Yea, in heart ye work wickedness; ye weigh out the violence of your hands in the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The wicked go astray from the womb; they err as soon as they are born, speaking lies.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Their poison is like the poison of a serpent: [they are] like the deaf adder which stoppeth her ear;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Which doth not hearken to the voice of enchanters, of one charming ever so wisely.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: OGod, break their teeth in their mouth; break out the great teeth of the young lions, O Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let them melt away as waters that flow off; when he aimeth his arrows, let them be as blunted:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let them be as a snail that melteth as it passeth away; [like] the untimely birth of a woman, let them not see the sun.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Before your pots feel the thorns, green or burning, — they shall be whirled away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The righteous shall rejoice when he seeth the vengeance; he shall wash his footsteps in the blood of the wicked:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And men shall say, Verily there is fruit for the righteous; verily there is aGod that judgeth in the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Deliver me from mine enemies, O myGod; secure me on high from them that rise up against me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Deliver me from the workers of iniquity, and save me from men of blood.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For behold, they lie in wait for my soul; strong ones are gathered against me: not for my transgression, nor for my sin, O Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They run and prepare themselves without [my] fault: awake to meet me, and behold.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Yea, do thou, Jehovah, theGod of hosts, theGod of Israel, arise to visit all the nations: be not gracious to any plotters of iniquity. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They return in the evening; they howl like a dog, and go round about the city:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, they belch out with their mouth; swords are in their lips: for who [say they] doth hear?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But thou, Jehovah, wilt laugh at them; thou wilt have all the nations in derision.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Their strength! ...I will take heed to thee; forGod is my high fortress.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: God, whose loving-kindness will come to meet me, —God shall let me see [my desire] upon mine enemies.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Slay them not, lest my people forget; by thy power make them wander, and bring them down, O Lord, our shield.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [Because of] the sin of their mouth, the word of their lips, let them even be taken in their pride; and because of cursing and lying which they speak.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Make an end in wrath, make an end, that they may be no more; that they may know thatGod ruleth in Jacob, unto the ends of the earth. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And in the evening they shall return, they shall howl like a dog, and go round about the city.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They shall wander about for meat, and stay all night if they be not satisfied.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But as for me, I will sing of thy strength; yea, I will sing aloud of thy loving-kindness in the morning; for thou hast been to me a high fortress, and a refuge in the day of my trouble.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Unto thee, my strength, will I sing psalms; forGod is my high fortress, theGod of my mercy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: OGod, thou hast cast us off, thou hast scattered us, thou hast been displeased: restore us again.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast made the earth to tremble, thou hast rent it: heal the breaches thereof; for it shaketh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast shewn thy people hard things; thou hast made us to drink the wine of bewilderment.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast given a banner to them that fear thee, that it may be displayed because of the truth, (Selah,)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: That thy beloved ones may be delivered. Save with thy right hand, and answer me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: God hath spoken in his holiness: I will exult, I will divide Shechem, and mete out the valley of Succoth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Gilead is mine, and Manasseh is mine, and Ephraim is the strength of my head; Judah is my law-giver;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Moab is my wash-pot; upon Edom will I cast my sandal; Philistia, shout aloud because of me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who will bring me into the strong city? who will lead me unto Edom?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [Wilt] not thou, OGod, who didst cast us off? and didst not go forth, OGod, with our armies?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Give us help from trouble; for vain is man's deliverance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: ThroughGod we shall do valiantly; and he it is that will tread down our adversaries.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hear, OGod, my cry; attend unto my prayer.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: From the end of the earth will I call unto thee, when my heart is overwhelmed: thou wilt lead me on to a rock which is too high for me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thou hast been a refuge for me, a strong tower from before the enemy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will sojourn in thy tent for ever; I will take refuge in the covert of thy wings. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thou, OGod, hast heard my vows; thou hast given [me] the inheritance of those that fear thy name.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou wilt add days to the days of the king: his years shall be as many generations.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He shall abide beforeGod for ever: bestow loving-kindness and truth, that they may preserve him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So will I sing forth thy name for ever, performing my vows from day to day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: UponGod alone doth my soul rest peacefully; from him is my salvation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He only is my rock and my salvation; my high fortress: I shall not be greatly moved.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How long will ye assail a man; will ye [seek], all of you, to break him down as a bowing wall or a tottering fence?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They only consult to thrust [him] down from his excellency; they delight in lies; they bless with their mouth, but in their inward part they curse. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: UponGod alone, O my soul, rest peacefully; for my expectation is from him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He only is my rock and my salvation; my high fortress: I shall not be moved.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: WithGod is my salvation and my glory; the rock of my strength, my refuge is inGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Confide in him at all times, ye people; pour out your heart before him:God is our refuge. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Men of low degree are only vanity; men of high degree, a lie: laid in the balance, they go up together [lighter] than vanity.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Put not confidence in oppression, and become not vain in robbery; if wealth increase, set not your heart upon it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Once hathGod spoken, twice have I heard this, that strength [belongeth] untoGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And unto thee, O Lord, [belongeth] loving-kindness; for thou renderest to every man according to his work.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: OGod, thou art myGod; early will I seek thee. My soul thirsteth for thee, my flesh languisheth for thee, in a dry and weary land without water:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: To see thy power and thy glory, as I have beheld thee in the sanctuary;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thy loving-kindness is better than life: my lips shall praise thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So will I bless thee while I live; I will lift up my hands in thy name.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My soul is satisfied as with marrow and fatness, and my mouth shall praise [thee] with joyful lips.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When I remember thee upon my bed, I meditate on thee in the night-watches:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thou hast been my help, and in the shadow of thy wings will I sing for joy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My soul followeth hard after thee: thy right hand upholdeth me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But those that seek my soul, to destroy [it], shall go into the lower parts of the earth;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They shall be given over to the power of the sword; they shall be the portion of foxes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the king shall rejoice inGod; every one that sweareth by him shall glory: for the mouth of them that speak lies shall be stopped.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hear, OGod, my voice in my plaint; preserve my life from fear of the enemy:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hide me from the secret counsel of evil-doers, from the tumultuous crowd of the workers of iniquity,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who have sharpened their tongue like a sword, [and] have aimed their arrow, a bitter word;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: That they may shoot in secret at the perfect: suddenly do they shoot at him, and fear not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They encourage themselves in an evil matter, they concert to hide snares; they say, Who will see them?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They devise iniquities: We have it ready, the plan is diligently sought out. And each one's inward [thought] and heart is deep.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: ButGod will shoot an arrow at them: suddenly are they wounded;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: By their own tongue they are made to fall over one another: all that see them shall flee away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all men shall fear, and shall declareGod's doing; and they shall wisely consider his work.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The righteous shall rejoice in Jehovah, and trust in him; and all the upright in heart shall glory.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Praise waiteth for thee in silence, OGod, in Zion; and unto thee shall the vow be performed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: O thou that hearest prayer, unto thee shall all flesh come.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Iniquities have prevailed against me: our transgressions, thou wilt forgive them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blessed is he whom thou choosest and causest to approach: he shall dwell in thy courts. We shall be satisfied with the goodness of thy house, of thy holy temple.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: By terrible things in righteousness wilt thou answer us, OGod of our salvation, thou confidence of all the ends of the earth, and of the distant regions of the sea. ...

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who by his strength established the mountains, being girded with power;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who stilleth the raging of the seas, the raging of their waves, and the tumult of the peoples.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they that dwell in the uttermost parts are afraid at thy tokens; thou makest the outgoings of the morning and evening to rejoice.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast visited the earth, thou hast watered it; thou greatly enrichest it: the river ofGod is full of water; thou providest their corn, when thou hast so prepared it:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou dost satiate its furrows, thou smoothest its clods, thou makest it soft with showers; thou blessest the springing thereof.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou crownest the year with thy goodness, and thy paths drop fatness:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They drop upon the pastures of the wilderness, and the hills are girded with gladness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The meadows are clothed with flocks, and the valleys are covered over with corn; they shout for joy, yea, they sing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Shout aloud untoGod, all the earth:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Sing forth the glory of his name, make his praise glorious;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Say untoGod, How terrible are thy works! because of the greatness of thy strength, thine enemies come cringing unto thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All the earth shall worship thee, and sing psalms unto thee: they shall sing forth thy name. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Come and see the works ofGod: he is terrible in [his] doings toward the children of men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He turned the sea into dry [land]; they went through the river on foot: there did we rejoice in him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He ruleth by his power for ever; his eyes observe the nations: let not the rebellious exalt themselves. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Bless ourGod, ye peoples, and make the voice of his praise to be heard;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who hath set our soul in life, and suffereth not our feet to be moved.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thou, OGod, hast proved us: thou hast tried us, as silver is tried.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou broughtest us into a net, thou didst lay a heavy burden upon our loins;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou didst cause men to ride over our head; we went through fire and through water: but thou hast brought us out into abundance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will go into thy house with burnt-offerings; I will perform my vows to thee,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Which my lips have uttered, and my mouth hath spoken, when I was in trouble.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will offer up unto thee burnt-offerings of fatted beasts, with the incense of rams; I will offer bullocks with goats. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Come, hear, all ye that fearGod, and I will declare what he hath done for my soul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I called unto him with my mouth, and he was extolled with my tongue.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Had I regarded iniquity in my heart, the Lord would not hear.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: ButGod hath heard; he hath attended to the voice of my prayer.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blessed beGod, who hath not turned away my prayer, nor his loving-kindness from me!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: God be gracious unto us, and bless us, [and] cause his face to shine upon us; Selah,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: That thy way may be known upon earth, thy salvation among all nations.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let the peoples praise thee, OGod, let all the peoples praise thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let the nations rejoice and sing for joy: for thou wilt judge the peoples equitably; and the nations upon earth, thou wilt guide them. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let the peoples praise thee, OGod; let all the peoples praise thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The earth will yield her increase;God, ourGod, will bless us:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: God will bless us; and all the ends of the earth shall fear him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: LetGod arise, let his enemies be scattered, and let them that hate him flee before him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As smoke is driven, thou wilt drive them away; as wax melteth before the fire, the wicked shall perish at the presence ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the righteous shall rejoice: they shall exult beforeGod and be glad with joy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Sing untoGod, sing forth his name; cast up a way for him that rideth in the deserts: his name is Jah; and rejoice before him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A father of the fatherless, and a judge of the widows, isGod in his holy habitation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: God maketh the solitary into families; those that were bound he bringeth out into prosperity: but the rebellious dwell in a parched [land].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: OGod, when thou wentest forth before thy people, when thou didst march through the wilderness — (Selah) —

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The earth trembled, the heavens also dropped at the presence ofGod, yon Sinai, at the presence ofGod, theGod of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou, OGod, didst pour a plentiful rain upon thine inheritance, and when it was weary thou strengthenedst it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thy flock hath dwelt therein: thou hast prepared in thy goodness, for the afflicted, OGod!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The Lord gives the word: great the host of the publishers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Kings of armies flee; they flee, and she that tarrieth at home divideth the spoil.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Though ye have lain among the sheepfolds, [ye shall be as] wings of a dove covered with silver, and her feathers with green gold.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When the Almighty scattered kings in it, it became snow-white as Zalmon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [As] mount Bashan is the mount ofGod, a many-peaked mountain, [as] mount Bashan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Why do ye look with envy, ye many-peaked mountains, upon the mount thatGod hath desired for his abode? yea, Jehovah will dwell [there] for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The chariots ofGod are twenty thousand, thousands upon thousands; the Lord is among them: it is a Sinai in holiness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast ascended on high, thou hast led captivity captive: thou hast received gifts in Man, and even [for] the rebellious, for the dwelling [there] of Jah Elohim.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blessed be the Lord: day by day doth he load us [with good], theGod who is our salvation. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: OurGod is theGod of salvation; and with Jehovah, the Lord, are the goings forth [even] from death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: VerilyGod will smite the head of his enemies, the hairy scalp of him that goeth on still in his trespasses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The Lord said, I will bring again from Bashan, I will bring [them] again from the depth of the sea;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: That thou mayest dip thy foot in blood: the tongue of thy dogs has its portion from enemies.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They have seen thy goings, OGod, the goings of myGod, my King, in the sanctuary.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The singers went before, the players on stringed instruments after, in the midst of maidens playing on tabrets.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In the congregations bless yeGod, the Lord, — [ye] from the fountain of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There is little Benjamin, their ruler; the princes of Judah, their company; the princes of Zebulun, the princes of Naphtali.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: ThyGod hath commanded thy strength: strengthen, OGod, that which thou hast wrought for us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Because of thy temple at Jerusalem shall kings bring presents unto thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Rebuke the beast of the reeds, the assembly of the strong, with the calves of the peoples: [every one] submitteth himself with pieces of silver. Scatter the peoples that delight in war.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Great ones shall come out of Egypt; Ethiopia shall quickly stretch out her hands untoGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye kingdoms of the earth, sing untoGod; sing psalms of the Lord, (Selah,)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Of him that rideth upon the heavens, the heavens which are of old: lo, he uttereth his voice, a mighty voice.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ascribe ye strength untoGod: his excellency is over Israel, and his strength is in the clouds.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Terrible art thou, OGod, out of thy sanctuaries, — theGod of Israel! He it is that giveth strength and might unto the people. Blessed beGod!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Save me, OGod; for the waters are come in unto [my] soul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I sink in deep mire, where there is no standing; I am come into the depths of waters, and the flood overfloweth me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I am weary with my crying, my throat is parched; mine eyes fail while I wait for myGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They that hate me without a cause are more than the hairs of my head; they that would destroy me, being mine enemies wrongfully, are mighty: then I restored that which I took not away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou, OGod, knowest my foolishness, and my trespasses are not hidden from thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let not them that wait on thee, Lord, Jehovah of hosts, be ashamed through me; let not those that seek thee be confounded through me, OGod of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Because for thy sake I have borne reproach; confusion hath covered my face.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I am become a stranger unto my brethren, and an alien unto my mother's sons;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the zeal of thy house hath devoured me, and the reproaches of them that reproach thee have fallen upon me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I wept, my soul was fasting: that also was to my reproach; —

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I made sackcloth my garment: then I became a proverb to them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They that sit in the gate talk of me, and [I am] the song of the drunkards.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But as for me, my prayer is unto thee, Jehovah, in an acceptable time: OGod, in the abundance of thy loving-kindness answer me, according to the truth of thy salvation:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Deliver me out of the mire, let me not sink; let me be delivered from them that hate me, and out of the depths of waters.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let not the flood of waters overflow me, neither let the deep swallow me up; and let not the pit shut its mouth upon me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Answer me, O Jehovah; for thy loving-kindness is good: according to the abundance of thy tender mercies, turn toward me;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And hide not thy face from thy servant, for I am in trouble: answer me speedily.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Draw nigh unto my soul, be its redeemer; ransom me because of mine enemies.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou knowest my reproach, and my shame, and my dishonour: mine adversaries are all before thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Reproach hath broken my heart, and I am overwhelmed: and I looked for sympathy, but there was none; and for comforters, but I found none.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Yea, they gave me gall for my food, and in my thirst they gave me vinegar to drink.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let their table become a snare before them, and their very welfare a trap;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let their eyes be darkened, that they see not, and make their loins continually to shake.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Pour out thine indignation upon them, and let the fierceness of thine anger take hold of them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let their habitation be desolate; let there be no dweller in their tents.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For they persecute him whom thou hast smitten, and they talk for the sorrow of those whom thou hast wounded.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Add iniquity unto their iniquity, and let them not come into thy righteousness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let them be blotted out of the book of life, and not be written with the righteous.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I am afflicted and sorrowful: let thy salvation, OGod, set me secure on high.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will praise the name ofGod with a song, and will magnify him with thanksgiving;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall please Jehovah more than an ox, — a bullock with horns and cloven hoofs.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The meek shall see it, they shall be glad; ye that seekGod, your heart shall live.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For Jehovah heareth the needy, and despiseth not his prisoners.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let heavens and earth praise him; the seas, and everything that moveth therein.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: ForGod will save Zion, and will build the cities of Judah; and they shall dwell there, and possess it:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the seed of his servants shall inherit it, and they that love his name shall dwell therein.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Make haste, OGod, to deliver me; Jehovah, [hasten] to my help.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let them be ashamed and brought to confusion that seek after my soul; let them be turned backward and confounded that take pleasure in mine adversity;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let them turn back because of their shame that say, Aha! Aha!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let all those that seek thee be glad and rejoice in thee, and let such as love thy salvation say continually, LetGod be magnified!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I am afflicted and needy: make haste unto me, OGod. Thou art my help and my deliverer: O Jehovah, make no delay.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In thee, Jehovah, do I trust: let me never be ashamed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Deliver me in thy righteousness, and rescue me; incline thine ear unto me, and save me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be to me a rock of habitation, whereunto I may continually resort: thou hast given commandment to save me; for thou art my rock and my fortress.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: MyGod, rescue me out of the hand of the wicked, out of the hand of the unrighteous and cruel man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thou art my hope, O Lord Jehovah, my confidence from my youth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: On thee have I been stayed from the womb; from the bowels of my mother thou didst draw me forth: my praise shall be continually of thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I have been as a wonder unto many; but thou art my strong refuge.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My mouth shall be filled with thy praise, with thy glory, all the day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Cast me not off in the time of old age; forsake me not when my strength faileth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For mine enemies speak against me, and they that watch for my soul consult together,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Saying,God hath forsaken him; pursue and seize him, for there is none to deliver.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: OGod, be not far from me; myGod, hasten to my help.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let them be ashamed, let them be consumed, that are adversaries to my soul; let them be covered with reproach and dishonour that seek my hurt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But as for me, I will hope continually, and will praise thee yet more and more.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My mouth shall declare thy righteousness, [and] thy salvation all the day: for I know not the numbers [thereof].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will go in the might of the Lord Jehovah; I will recall thy righteousness, thine alone.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: OGod, thou hast taught me from my youth, and hitherto have I proclaimed thy marvellous works:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now also, when I am old and greyheaded, OGod, forsake me not, until I have proclaimed thine arm unto [this] generation, thy might to every one that is to come.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thy righteousness, OGod, reacheth on high, thou who hast done great things: OGod, who is like unto thee?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou, who hast shewn us many and sore troubles, wilt revive us again, and wilt bring us up again from the depths of the earth;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou wilt increase my greatness, and comfort me on every side.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will also praise thee with the psaltery, even thy truth, myGod; unto thee will I sing psalms with the harp, thou holy One of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My lips shall exult when I sing psalms unto thee; and my soul, which thou hast redeemed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My tongue also shall talk of thy righteousness all the day; for they shall be ashamed, for they shall be brought to confusion, that seek my hurt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: OGod, give the king thy judgments, and thy righteousness unto the king's son.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He will judge thy people with righteousness, and thine afflicted with judgment.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The mountains shall bring peace to the people, and the hills, by righteousness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He will do justice to the afflicted of the people; he will save the children of the needy, and will break in pieces the oppressor.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They shall fear thee as long as sun and moon endure, from generation to generation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He shall come down like rain on the mown grass, as showers that water the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In his days shall the righteous flourish, and abundance of peace till the moon be no more.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he shall have dominion from sea to sea, and from the river unto the ends of the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The dwellers in the desert shall bow before him, and his enemies shall lick the dust.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The kings of Tarshish and of the isles shall render presents; the kings of Sheba and Seba shall offer tribute:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Yea, all kings shall bow down before him; all nations shall serve him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he will deliver the needy who crieth, and the afflicted, who hath no helper;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He will have compassion on the poor and needy, and will save the souls of the needy:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He will redeem their souls from oppression and violence, and precious shall their blood be in his sight.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he shall live; and to him shall be given of the gold of Sheba; and prayer shall be made for him continually: all the day shall he be blessed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There shall be abundance of corn in the earth, upon the top of the mountains; the fruit thereof shall shake like Lebanon; and they of the city shall bloom like the herb of the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His name shall endure for ever; his name shall be continued as long as the sun: and [men] shall bless themselves in him; all nations shall call him blessed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blessed be Jehovah Elohim, theGod of Israel, who alone doeth wondrous things!
