translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And blessed be his glorious name for ever! and let the whole earth be filled with his glory! Amen, and Amen.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The prayers of David the son of Jesse are ended.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: TrulyGod is good to Israel, to such as are of a pure heart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But as for me, my feet were almost gone, my steps had well nigh slipped;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I was envious at the arrogant, seeing the prosperity of the wicked.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For they have no pangs in their death, and their body is well nourished;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They have not the hardships of mankind, neither are they plagued like [other] men:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore pride encompasseth them as a neck-chain, violence covereth them [as] a garment;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Their eyes stand out from fatness, they exceed the imaginations of their heart:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They mock and speak wickedly of oppression, they speak loftily:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They set their mouth in the heavens, and their tongue walketh through the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore his people turn hither, and waters in fulness are wrung out to them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they say, How canGod know, and is there knowledge in the Most High?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, these are the wicked, and they prosper in the world: they heap up riches.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Truly have I purified my heart in vain, and washed my hands in innocency:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For all the day have I been plagued, and chastened every morning.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If I said, I will speak thus, behold, I should be faithless to the generation of thy children.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When I thought to be able to know this, it was a grievous task in mine eyes;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Until I went into the sanctuaries ofGod; [then] understood I their end.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Truly thou settest them in slippery places, thou castest them down in ruins.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How are they suddenly made desolate! they pass away, consumed with terrors.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As a dream, when one awaketh, wilt thou, Lord, on arising despise their image.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When my heart was in a ferment, and I was pricked in my reins,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then I was brutish and knew nothing; I was [as] a beast with thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Nevertheless I am continually with thee: thou hast holden my right hand;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou wilt guide me by thy counsel, and after the glory, thou wilt receive me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whom have I in the heavens? and there is none upon earth I desire beside thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My flesh and my heart faileth:God is the rock of my heart and my portion for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For behold, they that are far from thee shall perish; thou destroyest every one that goeth a whoring from thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But as for me, it is good for me to draw near toGod: I have put my trust in the Lord Jehovah, that I may declare all thy works.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Why, OGod, hast thou cast off for ever? [why] doth thine anger smoke against the sheep of thy pasture?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Remember thine assembly, which thou hast purchased of old, which thou hast redeemed [to be] the portion of thine inheritance, this mount Zion, wherein thou hast dwelt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Lift up thy steps unto the perpetual desolations: everything in the sanctuary hath the enemy destroyed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thine adversaries roar in the midst of thy place of assembly; they set up their signs [for] signs.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [A man] was known as he could lift up axes in the thicket of trees;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now they break down its carved work altogether, with hatchets and hammers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They have set on fire thy sanctuary, they have profaned the habitation of thy name to the ground.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They said in their heart, Let us destroy them together: they have burned up allGod's places of assembly in the land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: We see not our signs; there is no more any prophet, neither is there among us any that knoweth how long.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How long, OGod, shall the adversary reproach? Shall the enemy contemn thy name for ever?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Why withdrawest thou thy hand, and thy right hand? [pluck it] out of thy bosom: consume [them].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: ButGod is my king of old, accomplishing deliverances in the midst of the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou didst divide the sea by thy strength; thou didst break the heads of the monsters on the waters:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou didst break in pieces the heads of leviathan, thou gavest him to be meat to those that people the desert.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou didst cleave fountain and torrent, thou driedst up ever-flowing rivers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The day is thine, the night also is thine; thou hast prepared the moon and the sun:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast set all the borders of the earth; summer and winter — thou didst form them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Remember this, that an enemy hath reproached Jehovah, and a foolish people have contemned thy name.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Give not up the soul of thy turtle-dove unto the wild beast; forget not the troop of thine afflicted for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Have respect unto the covenant; for the dark places of the earth are full of the dwellings of violence.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Oh let not the oppressed one return ashamed; let the afflicted and needy praise thy name.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Rise up, OGod, plead thine own cause: remember how the foolish man reproacheth thee all the day;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Forget not the voice of thine adversaries: the tumult of those that rise up against thee ascendeth continually.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Unto thee we give thanks, OGod, we give thanks; and thy name is near: thy marvellous works declare it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When I shall receive the assembly, I will judge with equity.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The earth and all the inhabitants thereof are dissolved: I have established its pillars. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I said unto the boastful, Boast not; and to the wicked, Lift not up the horn:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Lift not up your horn on high; speak not arrogantly with a [stiff] neck.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For not from the east nor from the west, nor yet from the south doth exaltation [come]:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: ForGod is the judge; he putteth down one and exalteth another.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For in the hand of Jehovah there is a cup, and it foameth with wine, it is full of mixture; and he poureth out of the same; yea, the dregs thereof shall all the wicked of the earth drain off, [and] drink.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But as for me, I will declare for ever; I will sing psalms to theGod of Jacob.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the horns of the wicked will I cut off; [but] the horns of the righteous shall be exalted.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In Judah isGod known, his name is great in Israel;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And in Salem is his tent, and his dwelling-place in Zion.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There broke he the flashings of the bow, shield and sword and battle. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: More glorious art thou, more excellent, than the mountains of prey.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The stout-hearted are made a spoil, they have slept their sleep; and none of the men of might have found their hands.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: At thy rebuke, OGod of Jacob, both chariot and horse are cast into a dead sleep.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou, thou art to be feared, and who can stand before thee when once thou art angry?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou didst cause judgment to be heard from the heavens; the earth feared, and was still,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: WhenGod rose up to judgment, to save all the meek of the earth. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the fury of man shall praise thee; the remainder of fury wilt thou gird on thyself.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Vow and pay unto Jehovah yourGod: let all that are round about him bring presents unto him that is to be feared.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He cutteth off the spirit of princes; [he] is terrible to the kings of the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My voice is untoGod, and I will cry; my voice is untoGod, and he will give ear unto me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In the day of my trouble, I sought the Lord: my hand was stretched out in the night, and slacked not; my soul refused to be comforted.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I rememberedGod, and I moaned; I complained, and my spirit was overwhelmed. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou holdest open mine eyelids; I am full of disquiet and cannot speak.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I consider the days of old, the years of ancient times.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I remember my song in the night; I muse in mine own heart, and my spirit maketh diligent search.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Will the Lord cast off for ever? and will he be favourable no more?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hath his loving-kindness ceased for ever? hath [his] word come to an end from generation to generation?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: HathGod forgotten to be gracious? or hath he in anger shut up his tender mercies? Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then said I, This is my weakness: — the years of the right hand of the Most High

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Will I remember, — the works of Jah; for I will remember thy wonders of old,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will meditate upon all thy work, and muse upon thy doings.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: OGod, thy way is in the sanctuary: who is so great agod asGod?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou art theGod that doest wonders; thou hast declared thy strength among the peoples.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast with [thine] arm redeemed thy people, the sons of Jacob and Joseph. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The waters saw thee, OGod, the waters saw thee; they trembled, yea, the depths were troubled:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The thick clouds poured out water; the skies sent out a sound, yea, thine arrows went abroad:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The voice of thy thunder was in the whirlwind, lightnings lit up the world; the earth was troubled and it quaked.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thy way is in the sea, and thy paths are in the great waters; and thy footsteps are not known.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou leddest thy people like a flock by the hand of Moses and Aaron.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Give ear, O my people, to my law; incline your ears to the words of my mouth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will open my mouth in a parable; I will utter riddles from of old,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Which we have heard and known, and our fathers have told us:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: We will not hide [them] from their sons, shewing forth to the generation to come the praises of Jehovah, and his strength, and his marvellous works which he hath done.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he established a testimony in Jacob, and appointed a law in Israel, which he commanded our fathers, that they should make them known to their children;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: That the generation to come might know [them], the children that should be born; that they might rise up and tell [them] to their children,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And that they might set their hope inGod, and not forget the works ofGod, but observe his commandments;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And might not be as their fathers, a stubborn and rebellious generation, a generation that prepared not their heart, and whose spirit was not stedfast withGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The sons of Ephraim, armed bowmen, turned back in the day of battle.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They kept not the covenant ofGod, and refused to walk in his law;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And forgot his doings, and his marvellous works which he had shewn them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In the sight of their fathers had he done wonders, in the land of Egypt, the field of Zoan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He clave the sea, and caused them to pass through; and made the waters to stand as a heap;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he led them with a cloud in the daytime, and all the night with the light of fire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He clave rocks in the wilderness, and gave [them] drink as out of the depths, abundantly;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he brought streams out of the rock, and caused waters to run down like rivers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Yet they still went on sinning against him, provoking the Most High in the desert;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they temptedGod in their heart, by asking meat for their lust;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they spoke againstGod: they said, IsGod able to prepare a table in the wilderness?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, he smote the rock, and waters gushed out, and streams overflowed; is he able to give bread also, or provide flesh for his people?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore Jehovah heard, and was wroth; and fire was kindled against Jacob, and anger also went up against Israel:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Because they believed not inGod, and confided not in his salvation;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Though he had commanded the clouds from above, and had opened the doors of the heavens,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And had rained down manna upon them to eat, and had given them the corn of the heavens.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Man did eat the bread of the mighty; he sent them provision to the full.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He caused the east wind to rise in the heavens, and by his strength he brought the south wind;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he rained flesh upon them as dust, and feathered fowl as the sand of the seas,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he let it fall in the midst of their camp, round about their habitations:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they did eat, and were well filled; for that they lusted after, he brought to them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They were not alienated from their lust, their meat was yet in their mouths,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When the anger ofGod went up against them; and he slew the fattest of them, and smote down the chosen men of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For all this, they sinned still, and believed not in his marvellous works;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he consumed their days in vanity, and their years in terror.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When he slew them, then they sought him, and returned and sought early afterGod;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they remembered thatGod was their rock, andGod, the Most High, their redeemer.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But they flattered him with their mouth, and lied unto him with their tongue;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For their heart was not firm toward him, neither were they stedfast in his covenant.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he was merciful: he forgave the iniquity, and destroyed [them] not; but many a time turned he his anger away, and did not stir up all his fury:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he remembered that they were flesh, a breath that passeth away and cometh not again.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How oft did they provoke him in the wilderness, and grieve him in the desert!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they turned again and temptedGod, and grieved the Holy One of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They remembered not his hand, the day when he delivered them from the oppressor,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How he set his signs in Egypt, and his miracles in the field of Zoan;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And turned their rivers into blood, and their streams, that they could not drink;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He sent dog-flies among them, which devoured them, and frogs, which destroyed them;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he gave their increase unto the caterpillar, and their labour unto the locust;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He killed their vines with hail, and their sycamore trees with hail-stones;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he delivered up their cattle to the hail, and their flocks to thunderbolts.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He cast upon them the fierceness of his anger, wrath, and indignation, and distress, — a mission of angels of woes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He made a way for his anger; he spared not their soul from death, but gave their life over to the pestilence;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he smote all the firstborn in Egypt, the first-fruits of their vigour in the tents of Ham.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he made his own people to go forth like sheep, and guided them in the wilderness like a flock;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he led them safely, so that they were without fear; and the sea covered their enemies.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he brought them to his holy border, this mountain, which his right hand purchased;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he drove out the nations before them, and allotted them for an inheritance by line, and made the tribes of Israel to dwell in their tents.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But they tempted and provokedGod, the Most High, and kept not his testimonies,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they drew back and dealt treacherously like their fathers: they turned like a deceitful bow.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they provoked him to anger with their high places, and moved him to jealousy with their graven images.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: God heard, and was wroth, and greatly abhorred Israel:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he forsook the tabernacle at Shiloh, the tent where he had dwelt among men,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And gave his strength into captivity, and his glory into the hand of the oppressor;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And delivered up his people unto the sword, and was very wroth with his inheritance:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The fire consumed their young men, and their maidens were not praised in [nuptial] song;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Their priests fell by the sword, and their widows made no lamentation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then the Lord awoke as one out of sleep, like a mighty man that shouteth aloud by reason of wine;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he smote his adversaries in the hinder part, and put them to everlasting reproach.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he rejected the tent of Joseph, and chose not the tribe of Ephraim,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But chose the tribe of Judah, the mount Zion which he loved;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he built his sanctuary like the heights, like the earth which he hath founded for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he chose David his servant, and took him from the sheepfolds:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: From following the suckling-ewes, he brought him to feed Jacob his people, and Israel his inheritance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he fed them according to the integrity of his heart, and led them by the skilfulness of his hands.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: OGod, the nations are come into thine inheritance: thy holy temple have they defiled; they have laid Jerusalem in heaps.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The dead bodies of thy servants have they given to be meat unto the fowl of the heavens, the flesh of thy saints unto the beasts of the earth:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Their blood have they shed like water round about Jerusalem, and there was none to bury [them].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: We are become a reproach to our neighbours, a mockery and a derision to them that are round about us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How long, O Jehovah? wilt thou be angry for ever? Shall thy jealousy burn like fire?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Pour out thy fury upon the nations that have not known thee, and upon the kingdoms that call not upon thy name:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For they have devoured Jacob, and laid waste his habitation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Remember not against us the iniquities of [our] forefathers; let thy tender mercies speedily come to meet us: for we are brought very low.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Help us, OGod of our salvation, because of the glory of thy name; and deliver us, and forgive our sins, for thy name's sake.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wherefore should the nations say, Where is theirGod? Let the avenging of the blood of thy servants that is shed be known among the nations in our sight.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let the groaning of the prisoner come before thee; according to the greatness of thine arm, preserve those that are appointed to die;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And render unto our neighbours, sevenfold into their bosom, their reproach, wherewith they have reproached thee, O Lord.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And we, thy people and the sheep of thy pasture, will give thanks unto thee for ever; we will shew forth thy praise from generation to generation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Give ear, O Shepherd of Israel, thou that leadest Joseph like a flock; thou that sittest [between] the cherubim, shine forth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Before Ephraim and Benjamin and Manasseh, stir up thy strength, and come to our deliverance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: OGod, restore us; and cause thy face to shine, and we shall be saved.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah,God of hosts, how long will thine anger smoke against the prayer of thy people?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast fed them with the bread of tears, and given them tears to drink in large measure:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast made us a strife unto our neighbours, and our enemies mock among themselves.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Restore us, OGod of hosts; and cause thy face to shine, and we shall be saved.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou broughtest a vine out of Egypt; thou didst cast out the nations, and plant it:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou preparedst space before it, and it took deep root, and filled the land;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The mountains were covered with its shadow, and the branches thereof were [like] cedars ofGod;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It sent out its boughs unto the sea, and its shoots unto the river.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Why hast thou broken down its fences, so that all who pass by the way do pluck it?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The boar out of the forest doth waste it, and the beast of the field doth feed off it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: OGod of hosts, return, we beseech thee; look down from the heavens, and behold, and visit this vine;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Even the stock which thy right hand hath planted, and the young plant thou madest strong for thyself.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It is burned with fire, it is cut down; they perish at the rebuke of thy countenance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let thy hand be upon the man of thy right hand, upon the son of man whom thou hast made strong for thyself.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So will we not go back from thee. Revive us, and we will call upon thy name.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Restore us, O Jehovah,God of hosts; cause thy face to shine, and we shall be saved.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Sing ye joyously untoGod our strength, shout aloud unto theGod of Jacob;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Raise a song, and sound the tambour, the pleasant harp with the lute.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blow the trumpet at the new moon, at the set time, on our feast day:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For this is a statute for Israel, an ordinance of theGod of Jacob;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He ordained it in Joseph [for] a testimony, when he went forth over the land of Egypt, [where] I heard a language that I knew not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I removed his shoulder from the burden; his hands were freed from the basket.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou calledst in trouble, and I delivered thee; I answered thee in the secret place of thunder; I proved thee at the waters of Meribah. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hear, my people, and I will testify unto thee; O Israel, if thou wouldest hearken unto me!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There shall no strangegod be in thee, neither shalt thou worship any foreigngod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I am Jehovah thyGod, that brought thee up out of the land of Egypt: open thy mouth wide, and I will fill it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But my people hearkened not to my voice, and Israel would none of me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So I gave them up unto their own hearts' stubbornness: they walked after their own counsels.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Oh that my people had hearkened unto me, that Israel had walked in my ways!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I would soon have subdued their enemies, and turned my hand against their adversaries.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The haters of Jehovah would have come cringing unto him; but their time would have been for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he would have fed them with the finest of wheat; yea, with honey out of the rock would I have satisfied thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: God standeth in the assembly ofGod, he judgeth among thegods.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How long will ye judge unrighteously, and accept the person of the wicked? Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Judge the poor and the fatherless, do justice to the afflicted and the destitute;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Rescue the poor and needy, deliver them out of the hand of the wicked.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They know not, neither do they understand; they walk on in darkness: all the foundations of the earth are moved.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I have said, Ye aregods, and all of you are children of the Most High;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But ye shall die like men, and fall like one of the princes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Arise, OGod, judge the earth; for thou shalt inherit all the nations.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: OGod, keep not silence; hold not thy peace, and be not still, OGod:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For behold, thine enemies make a tumult; and they that hate thee lift up the head.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They take crafty counsel against thy people, and consult against thy hidden ones:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They say, Come, and let us cut them off from being a nation, and let the name of Israel be mentioned no more.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For they have consulted together with one heart: they have made an alliance together against thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The tents of Edom and the Ishmaelites, Moab and the Hagarites;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Gebal, and Ammon, and Amalek; Philistia, with the inhabitants of Tyre;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Asshur also is joined with them: they are an arm to the sons of Lot. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Do unto them as to Midian; as to Sisera, as to Jabin, at the torrent of Kishon:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who were destroyed at Endor; they became as dung for the ground.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Make their nobles as Oreb and as Zeeb; and all their chiefs as Zebah and as Zalmunna.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For they have said, Let us take to ourselvesGod's dwelling-places in possession.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: O myGod, make them like a whirling thing, like stubble before the wind.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As fire burneth a forest, and as the flame setteth the mountains on fire,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So pursue them with thy tempest, and terrify them with thy whirlwind.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Fill their faces with shame, that they may seek thy name, O Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let them be put to shame and be dismayed for ever, and let them be confounded and perish:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: That they may know that thou alone, whose name is Jehovah, art the Most High over all the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How amiable are thy tabernacles, O Jehovah of hosts!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My soul longeth, yea, even fainteth for the courts of Jehovah; my heart and my flesh cry out for the livingGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Yea, the sparrow hath found a house, and the swallow a nest for herself, where she layeth her young, thine altars, O Jehovah of hosts, my King and myGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blessed are they that dwell in thy house: they will be constantly praising thee. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blessed is the man whose strength is in thee, — they, in whose heart are the highways.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Passing through the valley of Baca, they make it a well-spring; yea, the early rain covereth it with blessings.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They go from strength to strength: [each one] will appear beforeGod in Zion.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah,God of hosts, hear my prayer; give ear, OGod of Jacob. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, OGod our shield, and look upon the face of thine anointed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For a day in thy courts is better than a thousand. I had rather stand at the threshold of the house of myGod, than dwell in the tents of wickedness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For Jehovah Elohim is a sun and shield: Jehovah will give grace and glory; no good thing will he withhold from them that walk uprightly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah of hosts, blessed is the man that confideth in thee!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast been favourable, Jehovah, unto thy land; thou hast turned the captivity of Jacob:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast forgiven the iniquity of thy people; thou hast covered all their sin. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast withdrawn all thy wrath; thou hast turned from the fierceness of thine anger.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Bring us back, OGod of our salvation, and cause thine indignation toward us to cease.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wilt thou be angry with us for ever? wilt thou draw out thine anger from generation to generation?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wilt thou not revive us again, that thy people may rejoice in thee?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Shew us thy loving-kindness, O Jehovah, and grant us thy salvation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will hear whatGod, Jehovah, will speak; for he will speak peace unto his people, and to hisgodly ones: but let them not turn again to folly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Surely his salvation is nigh them that fear him, that glory may dwell in our land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Loving-kindness and truth are met together; righteousness and peace have kissed each other:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Truth shall spring out of the earth, and righteousness shall look down from the heavens.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah also will give what is good, and our land shall yield its increase.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Righteousness shall go before him, and shall set his footsteps on the way.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Incline thine ear, Jehovah, answer me; for I am afflicted and needy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Keep my soul, for I amgodly; O thou myGod, save thy servant who confideth in thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be gracious unto me, O Lord; for unto thee do I call all the day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Rejoice the soul of thy servant; for unto thee, Lord, do I lift up my soul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thou, Lord, art good, and ready to forgive, and art of great loving-kindness unto all that call upon thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Give ear, O Jehovah, unto my prayer, and attend to the voice of my supplications.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In the day of my distress I will call upon thee, for thou wilt answer me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Among thegods there is none like unto thee, Lord, and there is nothing like unto thy works.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All nations whom thou hast made shall come and worship before thee, O Lord, and shall glorify thy name.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thou art great, and doest wondrous things: thou artGod, thou alone.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Teach me thy way, Jehovah; I will walk in thy truth: unite my heart to fear thy name.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will praise thee, O Lord myGod, with my whole heart; and I will glorify thy name for evermore.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For great is thy loving-kindness toward me, and thou hast delivered my soul from the lowest Sheol.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: OGod, the proud are risen against me, and the assembly of the violent seek after my soul, and they have not set thee before them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But thou, Lord, art aGod merciful and gracious, slow to anger, and abundant in goodness and truth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Turn toward me, and be gracious unto me; give thy strength unto thy servant, and save the son of thine handmaid.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Shew me a token for good, that they which hate me may see it, and be ashamed; for thou, Jehovah, hast helped me and comforted me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His foundation is in the mountains of holiness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah loveth the gates of Zion more than all the habitations of Jacob.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Glorious things are spoken of thee, O city ofGod. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will make mention of Rahab and Babylon among them that know me; behold Philistia, and Tyre, with Ethiopia: this [man] was born there.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And of Zion it shall be said, This one and that one was born in her; and the Most High himself shall establish her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah will count, when he inscribeth the peoples, This [man] was born there. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As well the singers as the dancers [shall say], All my springs are in thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah,God of my salvation, I have cried by day [and] in the night before thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let my prayer come before thee; incline thine ear unto my cry.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For my soul is full of troubles, and my life draweth nigh to Sheol.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I am reckoned with them that go down into the pit; I am as a man that hath no strength:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Prostrate among the dead, like the slain that lie in the grave; whom thou rememberest no more, and who are cut off from thy hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast laid me in the lowest pit, in dark places, in the deeps.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thy fury lieth hard upon me, and thou hast afflicted [me] with all thy waves. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast put my familiar friends far from me; thou hast made me an abomination unto them: I am shut up, and I cannot come forth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Mine eye consumeth by reason of affliction. Upon thee, Jehovah, have I called every day; I have stretched out my hands unto thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wilt thou do wonders to the dead? shall the shades arise and praise thee? Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Shall thy loving-kindness be declared in the grave? thy faithfulness in Destruction?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Shall thy wonders be known in the dark? and thy righteousness in the land of forgetfulness?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But as for me, Jehovah, I cry unto thee, and in the morning my prayer cometh before thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Why, O Jehovah, castest thou off my soul? [why] hidest thou thy face from me?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I am afflicted and expiring from my youth up; I suffer thy terrors, [and] I am distracted.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thy fierce anger hath gone over me; thy terrors have brought me to nought:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They have surrounded me all the day like water; they have compassed me about together.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Lover and associate hast thou put far from me: my familiar friends are darkness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will sing of the loving-kindness of Jehovah for ever; with my mouth will I make known thy faithfulness from generation to generation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I said, Loving-kindness shall be built up for ever; in the very heavens wilt thou establish thy faithfulness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I have made a covenant with mine elect, I have sworn unto David my servant:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thy seed will I establish for ever, and build up thy throne from generation to generation. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the heavens shall celebrate thy wonders, O Jehovah, and thy faithfulness in the congregation of the saints.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For who in the heaven can be compared to Jehovah? [who] among the sons of the mighty shall be likened to Jehovah?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: God is greatly to be feared in the council of the saints, and terrible for all that are round about him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah,God of hosts, who is like unto thee, the strong Jah? And thy faithfulness is round about thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou rulest the pride of the sea: when its waves arise, thou stillest them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast crushed Rahab as one that is slain; thou hast scattered thine enemies with the arm of thy strength.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thine are the heavens, the earth also is thine; the world and its fulness, thou hast founded them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The north and the south, thou hast created them: Tabor and Hermon triumph in thy name.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thine is the arm of might: strong is thy hand, high is thy right hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Righteousness and judgment are the foundation of thy throne; loving-kindness and truth go before thy face.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blessed is the people that know the shout of joy: they walk, O Jehovah, in the light of thy countenance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In thy name are they joyful all the day, and in thy righteousness are they exalted.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thou art the glory of their strength; and in thy favour our horn shall be exalted.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For Jehovah is our shield, and the Holy One of Israel, our king.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then thou spakest in vision of thy Holy One, and saidst, I have laid help upon a mighty one; I have exalted one chosen out of the people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I have found David my servant; with my holy oil have I anointed him:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: With whom my hand shall be established; and mine arm shall strengthen him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: No enemy shall exact upon him, nor the son of wickedness afflict him;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I will beat down his adversaries before his face, and will smite them that hate him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And my faithfulness and my loving-kindness shall be with him, and by my name shall his horn be exalted.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will set his hand in the sea, and his right hand in the rivers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He shall call unto me, Thou art my father, myGod, and the rock of my salvation;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as to me, I will make him firstborn, the highest of the kings of the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My loving-kindness will I keep for him for evermore, and my covenant shall stand fast with him;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will establish his seed for ever, and his throne as the days of heaven.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If his sons forsake my law, and walk not in mine ordinances;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If they profane my statutes, and keep not my commandments:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then will I visit their transgression with the rod, and their iniquity with stripes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Nevertheless my loving-kindness will I not utterly take from him, nor belie my faithfulness;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My covenant will I not profane, nor alter the thing that is gone out of my lips.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Once have I sworn by my holiness; I will not lie unto David:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His seed shall endure for ever, and his throne as the sun before me;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It shall be established for ever as the moon, and the witness in the sky is firm. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But thou hast rejected and cast off; thou hast been very wroth with thine anointed:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast made void the covenant of thy servant; thou hast profaned his crown to the ground:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast broken down all his hedges; thou hast brought his strongholds to ruin.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All that pass by the way plunder him; he is become a reproach to his neighbours.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast exalted the right hand of his oppressors; thou hast made all his enemies to rejoice:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Yea, thou hast turned back the edge of his sword, and hast not made him stand in the battle.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast made his brightness to cease, and cast his throne down to the ground;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The days of his youth hast thou shortened; thou hast covered him with shame. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How long, Jehovah, wilt thou hide thyself for ever? shall thy fury burn like fire?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Remember, as regards me, what life is. Wherefore hast thou created all the children of men to be vanity?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: What man liveth, and shall not see death? Shall he deliver his soul from the power of Sheol? Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Where, Lord, are thy former loving-kindnesses, [which] thou swarest unto David in thy faithfulness?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Remember, Lord, the reproach of thy servants — that I bear in my bosom [that of] all the mighty peoples —

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wherewith thine enemies, O Jehovah, have reproached, wherewith they have reproached the footsteps of thine anointed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blessed be Jehovah for evermore! Amen, and Amen.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Lord, thou hast been our dwelling-place in all generations.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Before the mountains were brought forth, and thou hadst formed the earth and the world, even from eternity to eternity thou artGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou makest [mortal] man to return to dust, and sayest, Return, children of men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For a thousand years, in thy sight, are as yesterday when it is past, and [as] a watch in the night.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou carriest them away as with a flood; they are [as] a sleep: in the morning they are like grass [that] groweth up:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In the morning it flourisheth and groweth up; in the evening it is cut down and withereth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For we are consumed by thine anger, and by thy fury are we troubled.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast set our iniquities before thee, our secret [sins] in the light of thy countenance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For all our days pass away in thy wrath: we spend our years as a [passing] thought.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The days of our years are threescore years and ten; and if, by reason of strength, they be fourscore years, yet their pride is labour and vanity, for it is soon cut off, and we fly away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who knoweth the power of thine anger? and thy wrath according to the fear of thee?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So teach [us] to number our days, that we may acquire a wise heart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Return, Jehovah: how long? and let it repent thee concerning thy servants.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Satisfy us early with thy loving-kindness; that we may sing for joy and be glad all our days.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Make us glad according to the days [wherein] thou hast afflicted us, according to the years [wherein] we have seen evil.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let thy work appear unto thy servants, and thy majesty unto their sons.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And let the beauty of Jehovah ourGod be upon us; and establish thou the work of our hands upon us: yea, the work of our hands, establish thou it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that dwelleth in the secret place of the Most High shall abide under the shadow of the Almighty.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I say of Jehovah, My refuge and my fortress; myGod, I will confide in him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Surely he shall deliver thee from the snare of the fowler, [and] from the destructive pestilence.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He shall cover thee with his feathers, and under his wings shalt thou find refuge: his truth is a shield and buckler.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou shalt not be afraid for the terror by night, for the arrow that flieth by day,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the pestilence that walketh in darkness, for the destruction that wasteth at noonday.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A thousand shall fall at thy side, and ten thousand at thy right hand; [but] it shall not come nigh thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Only with thine eyes shalt thou behold, and see the reward of the wicked.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Because thou hast made Jehovah, my refuge, the Most High, thy dwelling-place,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There shall no evil befall thee, neither shall any plague come nigh thy tent.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he shall give his angels charge concerning thee, to keep thee in all thy ways:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They shall bear thee up in [their] hands, lest thou dash thy foot against a stone.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou shalt tread upon the lion and the adder; the young lion and the dragon shalt thou trample under foot.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Because he hath set his love upon me, therefore will I deliver him; I will set him on high, because he hath known my name.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He shall call upon me, and I will answer him; I will be with him in trouble, I will deliver him and honour him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: With length of days will I satisfy him, and shew him my salvation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It is good to give thanks unto Jehovah, and to sing psalms unto thy name, O Most High;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: To declare thy loving-kindness in the morning, and thy faithfulness in the nights,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Upon an instrument of ten strings and upon the lute; upon the Higgaion with the harp.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thou, Jehovah, hast made me glad through thy work; I will triumph in the works of thy hands.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah, how great are thy works! Thy thoughts are very deep:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A brutish man knoweth not, neither doth a fool understand it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When the wicked spring as the grass, and when all the workers of iniquity flourish, it is that they may be destroyed for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou, Jehovah, art on high for evermore.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For lo, thine enemies, O Jehovah, for lo, thine enemies shall perish; all the workers of iniquity shall be scattered.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But my horn shalt thou exalt like a buffalo's: I shall be anointed with fresh oil.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And mine eye shall see [its desire] on mine enemies; mine ears shall hear [it] of the evil-doers that rise up against me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The righteous shall shoot forth like a palm-tree; he shall grow like a cedar on Lebanon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Those that are planted in the house of Jehovah shall flourish in the courts of ourGod:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They are still vigorous in old age, they are full of sap and green;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: To shew that Jehovah is upright: [he is] my rock, and there is no unrighteousness in him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah reigneth, he hath clothed himself with majesty: Jehovah hath clothed himself, he hath girded himself with strength; yea, the world is established, it shall not be moved.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thy throne is established of old; thou art from eternity.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The floods lifted up, O Jehovah, the floods lifted up their voice; the floods lifted up their roaring waves.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah on high is mightier than the voices of many waters, than the mighty breakers of the sea.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thy testimonies are very sure: holiness becometh thy house, O Jehovah, for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: OGod of vengeances, Jehovah,God of vengeances, shine forth;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Lift up thyself, thou judge of the earth, render the reward to the proud.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How long shall the wicked, O Jehovah, how long shall the wicked triumph?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [How long] shall they utter [and] speak insolence — all the workers of iniquity boast themselves?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They crush thy people, O Jehovah, and afflict thine inheritance;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They slay the widow and the stranger, and murder the fatherless,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And say, Jah will not see, neither will theGod of Jacob regard [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Understand, ye brutish among the people; and ye fools, when will ye be wise?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that planted the ear, shall he not hear? He that formed the eye, shall he not see?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that instructeth the nations, shall not he correct — he that teacheth man knowledge?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah knoweth the thoughts of man, that they are vanity.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blessed is the man whom thou chastenest, O Jah, and whom thou teachest out of thy law;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: That thou mayest give him rest from the days of evil, until the pit be digged for the wicked.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For Jehovah will not cast off his people, neither will he forsake his inheritance;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For judgment shall return unto righteousness, and all the upright in heart shall follow it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who will rise up for me against the evil-doers? who will stand for me against the workers of iniquity?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If Jehovah had not been my help, my soul had almost dwelt in silence.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When I said, My foot slippeth, thy loving-kindness, O Jehovah, held me up.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In the multitude of my anxious thoughts within me thy comforts have delighted my soul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Shall the throne of wickedness be united to thee, which frameth mischief into a law?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They band together against the soul of the righteous, and condemn innocent blood.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jehovah will be my high tower; and myGod the rock of my refuge.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he will bring upon them their iniquity, and will cut them off in their own evil: Jehovah ourGod will cut them off.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Come, let us sing aloud to Jehovah, let us shout for joy to the rock of our salvation;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let us come before his face with thanksgiving; let us shout aloud unto him with psalms.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For Jehovah is a greatGod, and a great king above allgods.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In his hand are the deep places of the earth; the heights of the mountains are his also:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The sea is his, and he made it, and his hands formed the dry [land].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Come, let us worship and bow down; let us kneel before Jehovah our Maker.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he is ourGod; and we are the people of his pasture and the sheep of his hand. To-day if ye hear his voice,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Harden not your heart, as at Meribah, as [in] the day of Massah, in the wilderness;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When your fathers tempted me, proved me, and saw my work.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Forty years was I grieved with the generation, and said, It is a people that do err in their heart, and they have not known my ways;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So that I swore in mine anger, that they should not enter into my rest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Sing ye unto Jehovah a new song: sing unto Jehovah, all the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Sing unto Jehovah, bless his name; publish his salvation from day to day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Declare his glory among the nations, his wondrous works among all the peoples.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For Jehovah is great and exceedingly to be praised; he is terrible above allgods.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For all thegods of the peoples are idols; but Jehovah made the heavens.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Majesty and splendour are before him; strength and beauty are in his sanctuary.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Give unto Jehovah, ye families of peoples, give unto Jehovah glory and strength;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Give unto Jehovah the glory of his name; bring an oblation and come into his courts;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Worship Jehovah in holy splendour; tremble before him, all the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Say among the nations, Jehovah reigneth! yea, the world is established, it shall not be moved; he will execute judgment upon the peoples with equity.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let the heavens rejoice, and let the earth be glad; let the sea roar, and the fulness thereof;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let the field exult and all that is therein. Then shall all the trees of the forest sing for joy,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Before Jehovah, for he cometh; for he cometh to judge the earth: he will judge the world with righteousness, and the peoples in his faithfulness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah reigneth: let the earth be glad, let the many isles rejoice.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Clouds and darkness are round about him; righteousness and judgment are the foundation of his throne.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A fire goeth before him, and burneth up his adversaries round about.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His lightnings lightened the world: the earth saw, and trembled.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The mountains melted like wax at the presence of Jehovah, at the presence of the Lord of the whole earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The heavens declare his righteousness, and all the peoples see his glory.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ashamed be all they that serve graven images, that boast themselves of idols. Worship him, all yegods.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Zion heard, and rejoiced; and the daughters of Judah were glad, because of thy judgments, O Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thou, Jehovah, art the Most High above all the earth; thou art exalted exceedingly above allgods.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye that love Jehovah, hate evil: he preserveth the souls of his saints, he delivereth them out of the hand of the wicked.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Light is sown for the righteous, and joy for the upright in heart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Rejoice in Jehovah, ye righteous; and give thanks in remembrance of his holiness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Sing ye unto Jehovah a new song: for he hath done wondrous things; his right hand and his holy arm hath wrought salvation for him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah hath made known his salvation: his righteousness hath he openly shewed in the sight of the nations.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He hath remembered his loving-kindness and his faithfulness toward the house of Israel: all the ends of the earth have seen the salvation of ourGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Shout aloud unto Jehovah, all the earth; break forth and shout for joy, and sing psalms.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Sing psalms unto Jehovah with the harp: with the harp, and the voice of a song;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: With trumpets and sound of cornet, make a joyful noise before the King, Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let the sea roar, and the fulness thereof; the world, and they that dwell therein;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let the floods clap [their] hands; let the mountains sing for joy together,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Before Jehovah, for he cometh to judge the earth: he will judge the world with righteousness, and the peoples with equity.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah reigneth: let the peoples tremble. He sitteth [between the] cherubim: let the earth be moved.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah is great in Zion, and he is high above all the peoples.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They shall praise thy great and terrible name, — it is holy! —

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the strength of the king that loveth justice. Thou hast established equity: it is thou that executest judgment and righteousness in Jacob.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Exalt Jehovah ourGod, and worship at his footstool. He is holy!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Moses and Aaron among his priests, and Samuel among them that call upon his name: they called unto Jehovah, and he answered them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He spoke unto them in the pillar of cloud: they kept his testimonies, and the statute that he gave them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah, ourGod, thou answeredst them: a forgivingGod wast thou unto them, though thou tookest vengeance of their doings.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Exalt Jehovah ourGod, and worship at the hill of his holiness; for holy is Jehovah ourGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Shout aloud unto Jehovah, all the earth!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Serve Jehovah with joy: come before his presence with exultation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Know that Jehovah isGod: it is he that hath made us, and not we ourselves; [we are] his people, and the sheep of his pasture.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Enter into his gates with thanksgiving [and] into his courts with praise; give thanks unto him, bless his name:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For Jehovah is good; his loving-kindness [endureth] for ever; and his faithfulness from generation to generation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will sing of loving-kindness and judgment: unto thee, Jehovah, will I sing psalms.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will behave myself wisely in a perfect way. When wilt thou come unto me? I will walk within my house in the integrity of my heart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will set no thing of Belial before mine eyes: I hate the work of them that turn aside; it shall not cleave to me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A perverse heart shall depart from me; I will not know evil.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whoso secretly slandereth his neighbour, him will I destroy; him that hath a high look and a proud heart will I not suffer.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Mine eyes shall be upon the faithful of the land, that they may dwell with me; he that walketh in a perfect way, he shall serve me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that practiseth deceit shall not dwell within my house; he that speaketh falsehoods shall not subsist in my sight.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Every morning will I destroy all the wicked of the land: to cut off all workers of iniquity from the city of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah, hear my prayer, and let my cry come unto thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hide not thy face from me: in the day of my trouble, incline thine ear unto me; in the day I call, answer me speedily.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For my days are consumed like smoke, and my bones are burned as a firebrand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My heart is smitten and withered like grass; yea, I have forgotten to eat my bread.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: By reason of the voice of my groaning, my bones cleave to my flesh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I am become like the pelican of the wilderness, I am as an owl in desolate places;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I watch, and am like a sparrow alone upon the housetop.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Mine enemies reproach me all the day; they that are mad against me swear by me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I have eaten ashes like bread, and mingled my drink with weeping,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Because of thine indignation and thy wrath; for thou hast lifted me up, and cast me down.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My days are like a lengthened-out shadow, and I, I am withered like grass.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But thou, Jehovah, abidest for ever, and thy memorial from generation to generation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou wilt rise up, thou wilt have mercy upon Zion: for it is the time to be gracious to her, for the set time is come.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thy servants take pleasure in her stones, and favour her dust.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the nations shall fear the name of Jehovah, and all the kings of the earth thy glory.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When Jehovah shall build up Zion, he will appear in his glory.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He will regard the prayer of the destitute one, and not despise their prayer.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This shall be written for the generation to come; and a people that shall be created shall praise Jah:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he hath looked down from the height of his sanctuary; from the heavens hath Jehovah beheld the earth,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: To hear the groaning of the prisoner, to loose those that are appointed to die;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: That the name of Jehovah may be declared in Zion, and his praise in Jerusalem,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When the peoples shall be gathered together, and the kingdoms, to serve Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He weakened my strength in the way, he shortened my days.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I said, MyGod, take me not away in the midst of my days! ...Thy years are from generation to generation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Of old hast thou founded the earth, and the heavens are the work of thy hands:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They shall perish, but thou continuest; and all of them shall grow old as a garment: as a vesture shalt thou change them, and they shall be changed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But thou art the Same, and thy years shall have no end.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The children of thy servants shall abide, and their seed shall be established before thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Bless Jehovah, O my soul; and all that is within me, [bless] his holy name!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Bless Jehovah, O my soul, and forget not all his benefits:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who forgiveth all thine iniquities, who healeth all thy diseases;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who redeemeth thy life from the pit, who crowneth thee with loving-kindness and tender mercies;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who satisfieth thine old age with good [things]; thy youth is renewed like the eagle's.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah executeth righteousness and justice for all that are oppressed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He made known his ways unto Moses, his acts unto the children of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah is merciful and gracious, slow to anger, and abundant in loving-kindness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He will not always chide, neither will he keep [his anger] for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He hath not dealt with us according to our sins, nor rewarded us according to our iniquities.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For as the heavens are high above the earth, so great is his loving-kindness toward them that fear him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As far as the east is from the west, so far hath he removed our transgressions from us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As a father pitieth [his] children, so Jehovah pitieth them that fear him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For himself knoweth our frame; he remembereth that we are dust.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As for man, his days are as grass; as a flower of the field, so he flourisheth:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the wind passeth over it, and it is gone, and the place thereof knoweth it no more.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the loving-kindness of Jehovah is from everlasting and to everlasting, upon them that fear him, and his righteousness unto children's children,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: To such as keep his covenant and to those that remember his precepts to do them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah hath established his throne in the heavens, and his kingdom ruleth over all.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Bless Jehovah, ye his angels, mighty in strength, that execute his word, hearkening unto the voice of his word.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Bless Jehovah, all ye his hosts; ye ministers of his that do his will.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Bless Jehovah, all his works, in all places of his dominion. Bless Jehovah, O my soul!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Bless Jehovah, O my soul! Jehovah myGod, thou art very great; thou art clothed with majesty and splendour;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Covering thyself with light as with a garment, stretching out the heavens like a tent-curtain; —

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who layeth the beams of his upper chambers in the waters, who maketh clouds his chariot, who walketh upon the wings of the wind;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who maketh his angels spirits; his ministers a flame of fire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He laid the earth upon its foundations: it shall not be removed for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hadst covered it with the deep, as with a vesture; the waters stood above the mountains:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: At thy rebuke they fled, at the voice of thy thunder they hasted away; —

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The mountains rose, the valleys sank, unto the place which thou hadst founded for them; —

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast set a bound which they may not pass over, that they turn not again to cover the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He sendeth the springs into the valleys: they run among the mountains;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They give drink to every beast of the field; the wild asses quench their thirst.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The birds of heaven dwell by them; they give forth their voice from among the branches.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He watereth the mountains from his upper-chambers: the earth is satisfied with the fruit of thy works.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He maketh the grass to grow for the cattle, and herb for the service of man; bringing forth bread out of the earth,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And wine which gladdeneth the heart of man; making [his] face shine with oil; and with bread he strengtheneth man's heart.
